Thought: I kind of get the idea that she's trying to protect some people, still... <<#Simple(10)>> ->What's on the hard drive, Fran? Frances Cheng: It's too complicated. I don't have time to explain! ->Tell me where you're going. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@芳說明她要去找嚴勝和對質哩>> Frances Cheng: I must go to HQ. Right now. Frances Cheng: I must see the CEO. This has to be a misunderstanding, a mistake... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: No, I can't convince myself. Frances Cheng: All documents on the hard drive point to the same thing... Frances Cheng: It's all real. Ms. Yeh's theories are all true. <<#GoTo(@芳深呼吸下定決心)>> <<#EndIf>> //沒放生葉宜樺或挺葉宜樺到底 Frances Cheng: I'm the only one who can do something...;<> <<#GoTo(@芳說明她要去找嚴勝和對質哩)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@芳深呼吸下定決心>> Frances Cheng: *Deep breath*;//芳擦掉眼淚，深呼吸 Frances Cheng: I must stop Vision from what it's doing.;<> Frances Cheng: It's not too late. Stop it before the damage is out of control. Thought: I absolutely have to find out what's going on. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Answer me, Fran! What's going on? ->Damage? Out of control? What scale are we talking about? Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I don't have time to explain the details. Frances Cheng: But Vision is potentially killing people in the tens or hundreds of thousands.;<> Frances Cheng: Or maybe they're already... No, I don't know that. Thought: Know what...? Frances Cheng: Anyway, I must put a stop to it, now.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Please, Jason, don't try to stop me. Frances Cheng: I will protect myself, and you, too. <<#Simple(6)>> ->You know I'm not worrying about myself. Frances Cheng: I do. But trust me. I will be OK. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I will take all possible precautions. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Let me go with you. Frances Cheng: No. I'm going to see the CEO. Frances Cheng: You can't go in, not without security clearance. ->Sleep on it. You will make rational decisions tomorrow. Frances Cheng: I don't have the time. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: You don't understand, J... Frances Cheng: I am their accomplice. They promised me, especially the CEO, he— Thought: Don't blame yourself again! Thought: You can't be responsible for all the bad things in the world! <<#Simple(15)>> ->This is none of your fault! ->They are they bad guys! Frances Cheng: I know! I know!;<> Frances Cheng: But I worked for them! Frances Cheng: I know it's not my fault. I never intended to hurt anyone. Frances Cheng: But the fact is, they used me to hurt people. Many people.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->In your current state, you will dive right into their trap. Frances Cheng: No, I'm not... Frances Cheng: I'm not mad at them... ->You're mad at yourself. Frances Cheng: I... Frances Cheng: Probably... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I must handle this on my own. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I have been gathering information and evidence for a long time... Thought: So, that was what she's been so busy with... Frances Cheng: I know their kind, their weak spots and dirty secrets.;<> Frances Cheng: They can't do me any harm. The stakes are too high. Frances Cheng: I guess this is the moment that I've always been preparing for.;<> //芳把電腦打開又看了下 Frances Cheng: This hard drive is the last piece of the puzzle. Frances Cheng: I will be OK, J. Frances Cheng: Now I know very well what I'm doing.;<> Frances Cheng: The worst case scenario, I lose my job, and get blacklisted by the whole industry.;<> Frances Cheng: Even so, I still have you. Right? Thought: Guess I can't stop her now...;<> Thought: Yet... Frances Cheng: If everything goes according to my plan, this will be over very soon.;<>;<> Frances Cheng: See you later. OK?;<> <> <<#Wait(1)>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Take care. Frances Cheng: I will. ->I love you. Frances Cheng: I love you, too. ->See you later. Frances Cheng: Yeah. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I'll be OK!;<> Frances Cheng: Relax! <> Thought: ...;<> Thought: This shouldn't have happened. Thought: She shouldn't be involved in this mess... Thought: ... Thought: But all I can do right now is to wait... Thought: ... Thought: I should get back to Screening... <<#End>>

title: 廁所神秘人
body: //第二天換衣服的時候他會進來洗臉看起來有點崩潰，然後時不時偷看主角，等主角靠近突然拉住主角 //一個神秘人會隨機順移到玩家附近，然後等玩家經過拉住主角。 //第一次沒有讓他認為主角是mbu，他之後還會出現，但下一次希望是玩家主動認出他 Woody Chen: Um, hey, wait... Woody Chen: Jason Dai?;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站摀臉返回) >> Thought: ...Who is this guy? Thought: He looks all strung up. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Do I know you? Woody Chen: Ugh, you can call me Chen.;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站低頭返回) >> ->Yes. Jason Dai. Woody Chen: Ni-nice to meet you...;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站低頭返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: You... Thought: He's staring hard into my eyes. What's going on? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Can I help you? ->Something funny with my face? Woody Chen: Ah, no, it's just... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: Um... Woody Chen: You... Woody Chen: Are you really you? Thought: His gaze is uncertain again... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Huh...? Woody Chen: Sorry, forget I asked.;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站低頭) >> Woody Chen: Never mind. I thought you were someone else. //小陳走掉了 <> Thought: What...? Thought: But he just called me by the name... Thought: Nah. Never mind the weirdos... <<#End>> ->No, I'm not really me. Woody Chen: I knew it...;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站低頭) >> Thought: ...Eh? <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: You must be confused, too... Woody Chen: It just happened. All of a sudden, I can't receive instructions anymore... Woody Chen: You're the same, right? Trapped inside.;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站低頭返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. You, too? Woody Chen: Yes. ->What the heck are you babbling about? Woody Chen: ... Thought: He's gaze is uncertain again... Woody Chen: You... Woody Chen: Never mind. I thought you were someone else.;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站低頭) >> //小陳走掉了 <> Thought: What...? Thought: But he just called me by the name... Thought: Nah. Never mind the weirdos... <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: I know you. We were on a mission together. Woody Chen: Do you remember me?;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站抬手) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nope. ->That must be someone else. <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: So, you don't remember me? Woody Chen: I mean, this me? Thought: What's "this me" supposed to mean? Woody Chen: Never mind. I will try the others.;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站抬手返回) >> Woody Chen: Something is wrong. We can't go back. Woody Chen: Something happened to the Source...;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站低頭) >> Woody Chen: I'm not even sure if I should talk to you. Woody Chen: No one knows when they're coming back. Woody Chen: Is this recorded? If yes... Woody Chen: ... Thought: Looks like he's scared by her own thoughts... Woody Chen: ...See you next time. I'll be in touch.;<< Play (陳木海, MBU_陳木海_工廠_側站低頭返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Can you find me? Woody Chen: ...Shouldn't be a problem. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Woody Chen: Bye. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Bye. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //小陳走掉了 <> Thought: Why did I waste so much time on him...?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站抬頭) >> Thought: I've never seen him before. Thought: Nah. Never mind the weirdos...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站抬頭返回) >> <<#End>>

title: 回篩選組辦公室
body:
//穩哥正在鄭緯座位問他問題

Wayne Zheng: Right. Delete his record, make sure the efficiency is 80% or better, then you're all good.
Tyler Cao: What about those below 80%?;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_正站抱胸低頭) >>
Wayne Zheng: Evaluate against the statistics of the same batch. File for premature disengagement only if there's an obvious gap.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外) >>
Tyler Cao: Same bat...
Tyler Cao: Ah! I see!;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_正站抱胸低頭返回) >>
Tyler Cao: You're a genius, Wayne!
Wayne Zheng: Haha. Your solution wasn't bad, either.
Tyler Cao: Really? I thought...;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_背坐左看) >>
Tyler Cao: Hm? Jason?;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_正站抱胸左看) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外返回) >>
Wayne Zheng: Hey, Jason! I was looking for you.
Wayne Zheng: Can you handle the rest, Tyler?;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_背坐左看返回) >>;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_正站抱胸左看返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外) >>
Tyler Cao: Sure, no problem!
Wayne Zheng: Good!
Wayne Zheng: Sorry to have you waiting, Jason.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_背坐左看) >>;<<Set($DLState,穩哥走掉囉)>>
Wayne Zheng: Sit down. Make yourself comfortable.;<< Play (鄭緯, MBU_鄭緯_背坐左看返回) >>
//鄭緯: 士傑，你坐啊。
//主角上椅子
<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外返回) >>
<<#End>>

title: 回去找鄭緯說決定
body:
Wayne Zheng: Um...;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約側坐看內)>>
Wayne Zheng: It's taken care of. Not as complicated as we thought.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站看外)>>
Wayne Zheng: Someone forgot to turn on the delivery system. Simple as that.
Jeff Wu: What an idiot.
Wayne Zheng: Hey, this someone is you!
Jeff Wu: Huh? No way! I thought it's Lawrence?
Wayne Zheng: Talking about Lawrence, has he responded?
//小蜂看手機
<< PlayUntil (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站看手機) >>
Jeff Wu: No.
Wayne Zheng: Keep calling. He can't just vanish during working hours.
Jeff Wu: Will do.
Wayne Zheng: Back to work. Chop chop.;<< Play (吳晉峰, MBU_吳晉峰_側站看手機返回) >>
Jeff Wu: Yessir!;<<Set($DLState,小峰乖乖回去工作了)>>
Wayne Zheng: Sorry to have you waiting.
Wayne Zheng: Made up your mind? Shall we go upstairs?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Maybe not. ($PromiseWuTalkAtTopFloor);//已經答應俊恩才出現
    Wayne Zheng: Oh, OK.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站看外返回)>>
    Thought: He looks disappointed.
    Wayne Zheng: Is it because you have something on the agenda?;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站看外)>>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->No, I'm just tired.
        Wayne Zheng: Right. Thank you for your hard work.
        Wayne Zheng: Go home. Have some rest.
        <<#GoTo(@拒絕阿偉不跟他去頂樓掰掰)>>
    ->Justin Wu wants to have a talk, too. I've decided to go with him.
        <<#GoTo(@告訴阿瑋自己已經跟俊恩約了耶)>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
->Justin Wu wants to have a talk, too. I've decided to go with him. ($PromiseWuTalkAtTopFloor);//已經答應俊恩才出現
    <<@告訴阿瑋自己已經跟俊恩約了耶>>
    <<Set($ToldWeiWuInvited,true)>>
    Wayne Zheng: ...
    Wayne Zheng: That was, hm, unfortunate, haha.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站看外返回)>>
    Thought: He's a little bit deflated.
    Wayne Zheng: Maybe next time.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站看外)>>
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Or, maybe we could all go there together?
        <<@找阿緯跟俊恩一起頂樓聊聊>>
        Wayne Zheng: Can we!?
        Thought: Wow, he's jumping at the proposal.
        Thought: Yet Justin said Wayne wouldn't want to.
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->I think it would be great.
            Wayne Zheng: Let's go, then!
            Thought: Is that really OK...?
            <<Set($TopFloorStiuationPath,3)>>
            <<#End>>
        ->Justin doesn't sound like a fan of the idea, though.
            Wayne Zheng: Huh, should have expected...
            Wayne Zheng: Never mind, then. Go see him now that you've made the appointment.
            Wayne Zheng: I have some work to do.
            Wayne Zheng: Have a good time!
            Thought: He looks hurt...
        <<#EndSimple>>
    ->Sure.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    <<@拒絕阿偉不跟他去頂樓掰掰>>
    Wayne Zheng: Bye.
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->Bye, Wayne.
        Wayne Zheng: Bye.
    ->Bye, boss.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    <<Set($TopFloorStiuationPath,2)>>
    <<#End>>
->Sure.
->I don't see why not.
    <<Set($TopFloorStiuationPath,1)>>
    //已經答應俊恩。
    <<#If($PromiseWuTalkAtTopFloor=1)>>
        Thought: Hold on. I already have an appointment with Justin!
        <<#Simple(6)>>
        ->Um, actually, I have an appointment with Justin Wu.
            Wayne Zheng: Oh! The same time?
            Wayne Zheng: Are you going to meet him?
            <<#Simple(99999)>>
            ->Yeah. I guess I should talk to him first.
                <<#GoTo(@告訴阿瑋自己已經跟俊恩約了耶)>>
            ->How about we go see him together?
                <<#GoTo(@找阿緯跟俊恩一起頂樓聊聊)>>
            ->Not really. Just thought you may want to know.
                Wayne Zheng: I see.
                Wayne Zheng: OK. If everything's settled, let's go.
                <<#End>>
            <<#EndSimple>>
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
        <<Set($TopFloorStiuationPath,4)>>
    <<#EndIf>>
    Wayne Zheng: Great. Let's go.
<<#EndSimple>>
<<#End>>

title: 俊恩打電話
body: Thought: How much longer am I supposed to wait here... Thought: What is it that Wayne wants to talk about...? //俊恩打電話 << PlayUntil (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約側坐通話) >> Justin Wu(c): Jason? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You have my number? Justin Wu(c): I have your profile. ->Yeah. Mr. Wu? Justin? Justin Wu(c): Right. <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): I'm calling to keep you updated about the juicer just now. Justin Wu(c): Um... Justin Wu(c): Sorry. I just realized, maybe you don't want to know? Thought: I don't know if I don't want to know... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Don't worry. I can handle it. //有拉拉桿 <<#If($DoPullEmergencyBar=true)>> Justin Wu(c): He lived. Thought: Thank gods... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Is he doing well? Justin Wu(c): Honestly, no. <<@俊恩白目直接說那人超慘的>> Justin Wu(c): Could be paralyzed for life, but at least he won't die. Thought: That might be worse than dropping dead... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Hope he recovers soon. Justin Wu(c): I don't think he ever will. And he won't be able to find a job, either. Thought: Hey... Justin Wu(c): Sorry, I was a bit too blunt... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Shit happens. Justin Wu(c): Yeah. ->Cruel world, it is. Justin Wu(c): R-right... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): ... ->That might be worse than dropping dead. Justin Wu(c): I agree. Thought: Guy's as blunt as an oak club... Justin Wu(c): But it was the result of his own choice. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I guess. Justin Wu(c): Then we should not be held responsible. ->I don't think so. Justin Wu(c): Is that so... Thought: He sounds a bit hesitant? <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Great! Justin Wu(c): Truth be told, he's not in a great condition. <<#GoTo(@俊恩白目直接說那人超慘的)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //沒拉拉桿 <<#If($DoPullEmergencyBar=false)>> Justin Wu(c): He died. Thought: Was it because of me? Because I didn't pull the emergency stop? <<#Simple(12)>> ->Because I didn't pull the emergency stop? Justin Wu(c): There is no evidence to establish your liability. Justin Wu(c): If you want my opinion, he'd still be dead even if you pulled the emergency stop. Thought: I detect uncertainty... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't worry about me. I can take the truth. Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): Let's just say, if you did pull the emergency stop, he'd have lost a lot less blood. Justin Wu(c): Considering the cause of death is blood loss... Justin Wu(c): But on the other hand, the medic said he could still end up dead, nonetheless. Justin Wu(c): All I want to say is that, Jason, no one can blame you for it. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thank you. ->Well, his family could. Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): Honestly, I'm speechless. I have no idea what to say right now. Justin Wu(c): Do you want to hear anything in particular? Thought: Unreasonably straightforward. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Say nothing. Justin Wu(c): OK. ->Say keep up the good work. Justin Wu(c): Keep up the good work. ->Say fuck this farm. Justin Wu(c): Fuck this farm. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I see. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): ...I'm sorry. <<#EndIf>> ->Right. I don't really want to know. Justin Wu(c): I understand. Justin Wu(c): Let's put that matter to rest, for now. <<#GoTo(@俊恩開始邀約主角頂樓咖啡)>> <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): ...That settles that, I guess. <<@俊恩開始邀約主角頂樓咖啡>> Justin Wu(c): And I'm calling you for something else, too. Justin Wu(c): Can we have a talk, in person, one on one? <<@俊恩頂樓的邀約>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Now? ($AskWuNowTopFloor);//選過消失 <> Justin Wu(c): Yeah. Justin Wu(c): What do you say? <<#GoTo(@俊恩頂樓的邀約)>> ->Wayne just said he wants to have a talk with me, too. Wayne Zheng. Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): Maybe next time, then. <<#Simple(12)>> ->Or, shall we three have an open conversation? Justin Wu(c): Um... Justin Wu(c): Wayne wouldn't agree. Justin Wu(c): And I'm not very inclined, either. Justin Wu(c): It doesn't matter. Maybe next time? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I can turn him down. <<#GoTo(@俊恩咖啡局說拒絕阿瑋)>> ->Yeah, next time. <<#GoTo(@俊恩咖啡局說下次)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I can turn him down. <<@俊恩咖啡局說拒絕阿瑋>> Justin Wu(c): Is that really OK? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not a big problem. ->You have the priority. Justin Wu(c): Really? Justin Wu(c): I'm surprised. People generally like Wayne better. Justin Wu(c): I know I'm the boring kind, so... <<#Simple(8)>> ->I want to learn more about Ops. Justin Wu(c): Ah, I see... Justin Wu(c): Sure thing. ->Come on, it's a talk, not a date. Man to man. Justin Wu(c): Oh, I don't mean that. I actually have a crush on somebody... Thought: Um, that's more than I need/want to know...? Justin Wu(c): Anyway, I hear you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): ... <<#GoTo(@答應俊恩去頂樓聊聊)>> ->You're right. He asked first. Justin Wu(c): No worries. Maybe next time. <<#EndSimple>> ->Yeah, next time. <<@俊恩咖啡局說下次>> Justin Wu(c): OK. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): Bye. <<#Simple(6)>> ->See you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> << Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約側坐通話返回) >> <<#End>> ->What are we gonna talk about? ($AskWuDiscussWhatOnTopFloor);//選過刪掉 <> Justin Wu(c): About this farm. <<#GoTo(@俊恩頂樓的邀約)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<@答應俊恩去頂樓聊聊>> <> Justin Wu(c): Let's meet on the roof. I will grant you elevator access. Justin Wu(c): See you later on the roof.;//具嗯會先去頂樓等 <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why the roof? Justin Wu(c): Nice view. Free of unwanted ears, too. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Saw the view. Nice indeed. Justin Wu(c): You've been there? Justin Wu(c): How? Thought: Oops, I forgot I don't have the security clearance. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I don't quite want to say. Justin Wu(c): No problem. Won't ask. Thought: I thought he'd press further... ->A friend took me there. Justin Wu(c): You know someone in the senior management? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sort of. Justin Wu(c): I see. ->Yes. My girlfriend. Justin Wu(c): I see. Thought: I thought he'd be more surprised... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): See you on the roof. ->Sounds great. Justin Wu(c): It is great. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Sure. Thanks. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): See you later. <<#Simple(6)>> ->See you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> << PlayUntil (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約側坐通話返回) >> <<#End>>

title: 頂樓阿緯與俊恩開頭分岐
body: //TopFloorStiuationPath頂樓情境，1=跟阿偉去、2=跟俊恩去、3=跟阿偉去，但有說答應俊恩、 4=跟阿偉去，但沒說答應俊恩 //俊恩再等，可能4=直接沒提已經答應俊恩了 3=有提到俊恩但沒有提他不喜歡所以阿偉跟過來了 <<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=4||$TopFloorStiuationPath=3)>> Justin Wu: Hm? Justin Wu: ... //4.直接沒提已經答應俊恩了 <<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=4)>> Wayne Zheng: Justin? What are you doing here? Justin Wu: I was about to ask the same. <<#EndIf>> //3.有提到俊恩但沒有提他不喜歡所以阿偉跟過來了 <<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=3)>> Justin Wu: Wayne...? Justin Wu: What are you doing here? Wayne Zheng: Ah, I... <<#EndIf>> Justin Wu: Not again... Justin Wu: Don't even start!;<> Wayne Zheng: Start what? I haven't said anything!;<> Justin Wu: You're peddling your nonsense again! Justin Wu: This is between me and Jason. What made you think you're welcome? //4.直接沒提已經答應俊恩了 <<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=4)>> Wayne Zheng: What are you talking about?;<> Wayne Zheng: I had no idea you're here, OK? Justin Wu: Oh, save it, puh-lease.;<> Justin Wu: You did exactly the same thing last week, when I was talking to Cathy. Wayne Zheng: I did ask her to bring me along, alright, but— <<#EndIf>> //3.有提到俊恩但沒有提他不喜歡所以阿偉跟過來了 <<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=3)>> Wayne Zheng: I really didn't intend to wedge myself between you and Jason. If you insist, I'll lea— Justin Wu: Leave. Now. Wayne Zheng: Wait, listen— <<#EndIf>> Justin Wu: Enough! I've had enough!;<> Wayne Zheng: No! I've had enough!;<> Wayne Zheng: What the hell is your problem?;<> Wayne Zheng: Why don't you just shut up and listen for once? Justin Wu: Why should I listen to your bullshit over and over again? Justin Wu: Enough is enough. No more!;<> Wayne Zheng: You son of...;<> Justin Wu: Sorry, Jason. Let's talk another day.;<> Wayne Zheng: You're running away again. Just stop and hear me out... <> //俊恩走調 <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> //1.純阿偉路線，跟阿偉一起搭電梯到頂樓，阿偉才想起來忘了買飲料 <<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=1)>> Wayne Zheng: How is it? Wayne Zheng: Nice view, huh? Wayne Zheng: The roof is pretty much deserted at night.;<> Wayne Zheng: I like to come up here and have a cup of... Wayne Zheng: Gah! I forgot to get coffee!;<> Thought: I forgot about it, too... Wayne Zheng: Would you wait here a minute? I'll go get coffee.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sure. Thank you. Thought: Having my superior buy me a cup of coffee sounds wrong... Wayne Zheng: Will be right back. Wait for me over there.;<> ->We could go together. Wayne Zheng: No need. Just wait for me here. Wayne Zheng: Wait for me over there.;<> ->Don't bother. Wayne Zheng: No, I insist!;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Okay. Thank you. Wayne Zheng: Sure! ->You really don't have to. Wayne Zheng: You mean it?;<> Wayne Zheng: Okay, then. Sorry... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Nothing to feel sorry for. I forgot to get coffee, too. Wayne Zheng: Hm, we're even. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: He's really going out of his way to be a nice guy...;<> <<#GoTo(@阿偉沒去買咖啡路線)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Will be right back. Wait for me over there. <<#EndSimple>> //阿偉小跑離開 <> Thought: So he goes, he's off to get coffee... Thought: A very hands-on team lead. Probably too much for his own good... Thought: But he's really nice to everyone. <<#End>> <<@阿偉沒去買咖啡路線>> Wayne Zheng: Let's move over there. <> <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> //2.純俊恩路線，到頂樓發現俊恩已經再等了，飲料買好了 //<<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=2)>> // 吳俊恩: 嗨！！！ // 吳俊恩: ……在、在這裡。;<> // 想法: 他好像覺得自己叫太大聲了，有點不好意思。 // <<#Simple(8)>> // ->嗨！！！ // 想法: 他笑了。 // ->你好。 // ->……… // <<#EndSimple>> // <> // //<<#EndIf>>

title: 頂樓阿緯路線
body: <<#If($DLState=阿緯買完咖啡開跑回來)>> Wayne Zheng: Here you are.;<>;<> ;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thank you. ->Appreciated. Wayne Zheng: You're welcome!;<> <<#GoTo(@瑋哥買咖啡有說謝謝)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: Why the sudden stop? Thought: Ah, he's expecting a thank you. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Thank you. Wayne Zheng: You're welcome! ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<@瑋哥買咖啡有說謝謝>> Wayne Zheng: Okay! Now we have the coffee sorted out...;<> Wayne Zheng: Let's get to business. <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: I come up here when the stress is too high. Wayne Zheng: The sea breeze is soothing. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Justin probably does the same. <<#Simple(10)>> ->How do you know? Wayne Zheng: Because we're friends! ->Does what? Wayne Zheng: Come up here to chill. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: This spot used to be our rendezvous, heh... Thought: Not a happy chuckle. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Guess I should cut to the chase.;<> Wayne Zheng: Tell me, Jason. Wayne Zheng: What do you think?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->About Justin? Wayne Zheng: Yeah. Wayne Zheng: You could say that. Actually I meant what do you think about Ops. ->You mean Ops? Wayne Zheng: Right. I'd like to know how you feel about Ops. ->Think, about what? Wayne Zheng: Sorry, my bad. Wayne Zheng: I'd like to know how you feel about Ops. <<#EndSimple>> Thought: Feel? <<@阿瑋問主角覺得操作組贊不贊壓>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not bad. Bunch of hardworking people who care about their work. Wayne Zheng: Why do you call them hardworking? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Because they care a lot about procedures. Wayne Zheng: Care about procedures? Or they don't care about the people? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You could say that. ->I don't think they are like that. Wayne Zheng: Do you...? <<#EndSimple>> ->They care quite a lot about the people sent down by Screening. Wayne Zheng: So they say.;<> Thought: Sarcasm from Wayne? That's rare... Wayne Zheng: Let me guess. They told you faulty machines are hurting contributors?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. Wayne Zheng: Still adhering to the old story, huh... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Do you mean the machines are not faulty? Wayne Zheng: They are faulty. Wayne Zheng: But they are not the worst danger to the contributors. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->It's not true? Wayne Zheng: Of course not true. Wayne Zheng: At least, not entirely true. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->A terrible place. Hazards everywhere. Wayne Zheng: Hazards? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Machines there are very outdated. Wayne Zheng: I know. This farm isn't exactly new. ->Their division lead doesn't care a thing about contributor safety. Wayne Zheng: Right. He has a reputation as a Grade A asshole. <<#EndSimple>> ->I don't feel anything in particular. Wayne Zheng: Huh... ->What do you mean by "feel"? ($AskWeiWhatFeeling);//選過拿掉 <> Wayne Zheng: Em, the general feeling.;<> Wayne Zheng: You have met their people and done their work. Wayne Zheng: What do you think?;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why ask? Wayne Zheng: Tell me. I'll explain later. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Anything to share? <<#GoTo(@阿瑋問主角覺得操作組贊不贊壓)>> <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Good. Thank you. Wayne Zheng: I just want to make sure what Ops is like these days. Wayne Zheng: You just came back from there. Thought I should ask.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I was there for only one day. Maybe I'm not the best source of information? Wayne Zheng: You are not my only source. And I deal with them myself, from time to time. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why do you ask, then? Wayne Zheng: I need to confirm...;<> Wayne Zheng: Confirm that nothing has changed. <> ->What is it like, working as part of their team? Wayne Zheng: Not pleasant. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Why don't you go down there and see for yourself? Wayne Zheng: I can't, not without a good reason. You should know that.;<> Wayne Zheng: But fundamentally, maybe I don't want to. <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why? Wayne Zheng: Because I really don't like it there. Wayne Zheng: Both the place, and the people. ->I dig you. Wayne Zheng: I figured. Having seen their worst side on your first day... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Soon, this farm will undergo some significant change. What happened to Ms. Yeh today is probably part of it.;<> Wayne Zheng: I guess you must have overheard here or there, about HQ planning to shut Silencio down.;<> Wayne Zheng: As the lead of Screening, I must make necessary preparations.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->Do you support HQ in this? Farm closure? Wayne Zheng: Of course not! <<@問阿瑋為啥不支持把廠廢一廢咧>> Wayne Zheng: You have done our routine. You know how those poor people need us for survival.;<> Wayne Zheng: Don't you think flop farms are the only solution in the age of mass unemployment? Not much of a solution, though. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->True. Wayne Zheng: We are the last straw for the poor.;<> ->Even if Silencio is shut down, they can still go to other farms, no? Wayne Zheng: No. The overwhelming majority of flop farms are running at full capacity.;<> Wayne Zheng: A few new farms are being built, but they are still years away. Wayne Zheng: We are the last straw for the poor. ->I wouldn't call being racked up and knocked out a way of survival. Wayne Zheng: I know what you mean. It's not like they have any other alternative.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: No matter how pathetic it is, we can't rob them of this last option.;<> Wayne Zheng: We should fix Silencio. Find a balance point. Not close shop and call it a day. Wayne Zheng: Or so I believe.;<> ->What does that have to do with Ops? Wayne Zheng: Because they are pushing for farm closure, against us. <<#GoTo(@問阿瑋為啥不支持把廠廢一廢咧)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: HQ will make its move very soon, putting farm closure on the agenda. Wayne Zheng: The debate will involve Ms. Yeh, me, and the lead of Ops.;<> Wayne Zheng: Before that day, I must gather as much information as possible to support my argument. Wayne Zheng: This flop farm cannot—will not—close. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Can I help you in any way? Wayne Zheng: Honestly, you can't. But maybe I can gain some insight from you.;<> Wayne Zheng: Answering my questions would help me find out what's going on at Ops these days. Thought: Vague questions like those...?;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: It's no secret that Ops wants Silencio closed. Originally I thought it was their division lead's idea. Wayne Zheng: Later I found out Justin is part of their gang. That came as a surprise... Wayne Zheng: Did they mention anything about farm closure during your field practice?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->They did, saying the machines are beyond repair. Wayne Zheng: The same story every time. ->Don't think they did. Wayne Zheng: Is that so? Wayne Zheng: Right. I guess he's not desperate enough to try to convert a new employee yet.;<> Thought: But you are trying to convert me. <> <<#Simple(6)>> ->But you are trying to convert me, no? Thought: Ouch. I should have padded the question a little bit... Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: I wouldn't call it "covert". Wayne Zheng: I'm providing you with necessary information.;<> Wayne Zheng: As to what to make of it, the decision is yours. I respect you as a grown man. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: The whole Ops, Justin included, always try to kill the discussion with professionalism.;<> Wayne Zheng: They are domain specialists, which gives them the privilege to lecture us. Wayne Zheng: All our opinions are brushed off as "ignorant daydreams".;<> Wayne Zheng: In their world, discussion is not necessary at all. They have all the answers already. Thought: ...;<> <> Wayne Zheng: Speaking of, I forgot to ask about your position.;<> Wayne Zheng: As an unbiased factor, do you think Silencio should be shut down? Thought: Hey, how can I say yes, after that much lobbying you just unloaded on me...;<> //這裡要設計幾個玩家立場:1.留廠2.廢廠3.全廢？4.不知道、不想搞事(相對中立) <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I think it must go. Thought: He froze for a split second. Wayne Zheng: May I know why? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The Ops people are right. Their machines are beyond saving. <<@是操作組想誤導你說機器爛爆好嗎>> Wayne Zheng: You are misled.;<> Wayne Zheng: Machines break down all the time. It's just that they refuse to properly service them. Thought: ...What? That's different from Justin's side of the story. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Can you prove it? Wayne Zheng: I can. ->But they said HQ doesn't allocate enough budget. Wayne Zheng: They use the same excuse every time. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: All this talk about budget shortage, never mentioning how much salary they take.;<> Wayne Zheng: HQ isn't exactly generous, but they never cut Silencio's budget. Each division has a reliable source of fund.;<> Wayne Zheng: The budget is fixed, but they keep giving themselves salary raise. You see where I'm going? Thought: Fixed budget, increasing salaries, meaning less money to spend on everything else...;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I see. But is that true? Wayne Zheng: Budget sheets are confidential, but not top secret. As a division lead, I have access to them.;<> Wayne Zheng: So do Ms. Yeh and Edith. We see the same thing going on year after year. ->In that case, what they said doesn't make sense. Wayne Zheng: You got it. The whole story is intended to mislead. Wayne Zheng: Those who can't see the big picture are easily tricked.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I hate to think it, but sometimes I have to, that... Wayne Zheng: That Justin, getting used to fat paychecks at Ops, have decided to join their side.;<> Wayne Zheng: Originally, I thought it's just their fat ass lead. Now I have reason to believe Ops, as a whole, are in it together. Wayne Zheng: Some of them might have been decent people, but money corrupts. Thought: Then why are they trying to destroy the source of their salary...? <<#Simple(10)>> ->In that case, shouldn't they defend the farm? Thought: A long sigh... ->I can't believe it. Wayne Zheng: It is the truth. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Because the situation is about to pass the tipping point.;<> Wayne Zheng: Increasing salaries, decreasing maintenance budget. Eventually, the Cellar starts to fall apart. Wayne Zheng: More contributors are injured or killed.;<> Wayne Zheng: You should have seen enough of it. <<#Simple(8)>> ->They are trying to cover it up? Wayne Zheng: Not really. There's nothing to cover. Wayne Zheng: Believe it or not, what they did is allowed by the company and the laws. No one is responsible for these "unfortunate accidents". ->Don't they worry about unemployment? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Vision spent serious money on their training. They will most likely be transferred to other farms. Wayne Zheng: Unemployment is unlikely. They may even bargain for a better package, using their current salary for leverage! Wayne Zheng: Then the loop starts all over again!;<> Thought: He's increasingly worked up... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Hey, calm down a little. Wayne Zheng: I mean! Wayne Zheng: I... Wayne Zheng: Sorry. That was not intended for you. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't worry. I know how you feel. Wayne Zheng: Thank you.;//檢定好感度？ ->Losing control is counterproductive. Wayne Zheng: I know. I just can't, when the subject is brought up... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: What I'm more concerned about is, maybe the same is happening across all flop farms?;<> Wayne Zheng: Like a disease, not yet discovered? Wayne Zheng: What do you think, Jason?;<> Thought: Um... Thought: It could be either a real issue, or just office politics...;<> Thought: If it's the latter, they shouldn't bother with a newbie's opinion. Thought: Plus, Wayne sounds earnest. I do believe he's a good man... Thought: What shall I tell him?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I don't know what to say. <<@說不想參與所以阿瑋表示諒解>> Wayne Zheng: Right. What I unloaded on you could be overwhelming.;<> Wayne Zheng: It's natural that you feel confused. Thought: ... <<@傾向支持阿瑋路線收尾部分>> Wayne Zheng: Please think about what I said, if you're recruited into Screening.;<> Wayne Zheng: If you pass the test on both sides, personally I hope you'd choose Screening.;<> Wayne Zheng: Unprecedented changes are coming. We could use more people. Wayne Zheng: Trustworthy, good people.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->What difference do I make? Wayne Zheng: I don't know, to be honest. <<@阿緯講評主角觀察時間。>> Wayne Zheng: But so far as I've seen, especially in the contract room...;//都先只以簽約判斷 //自私路線 <<#If($WhatBenefitTold<2&&$EndingChungWeiScoreCoLin<5)>> //張可霖路線3=幫可霖但不幫劉漢銘，所以算中庸路線 <<#If($EndingChungWeiScoreCoLin=3)>> <<#GoTo(@阿緯基本覺得你中庸中庸啦)>> <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: I can tell you're a helpful soul.;<> Wayne Zheng: Or, you care about your own peace of mind. Thought: He speaks true. Wayne Zheng: Don't get me wrong. I mean, you are practical, putting values into action.;<> Wayne Zheng: I always talk about helping people, but I don't count on slogans to change things. Wayne Zheng: I need a man or woman of action. You are exactly the right type. <<#GoTo(@頂樓事件以阿偉講評主角觀察結尾)>> <<#EndIf>> //友善路線 <<#If($WhatBenefitTold>1&&$EndingChungWeiScoreCoLin=5)>> Wayne Zheng: You care about others, and are willing to offer help, even at your own cost. Wayne Zheng: Those favorable terms you offered Hank Liu yesterday prove my point.;<> //有觸發張可霖感人內心話環節 <<#If($EndingChangCoLin = 4)>> Wayne Zheng: And you are considerate in your approach. Being able to befriend Karin Chang is no easy feat. <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: I can't say what you can do yet, but having someone like you around in hard times is always good. Wayne Zheng: I mean every word I said, Jason.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Thank you. Wayne Zheng: No. I should thank you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@頂樓事件以阿偉講評主角觀察結尾)>> <<#EndIf>> //中庸路線 <<@阿緯基本覺得你中庸中庸啦>> Wayne Zheng: I don't know what to make of you. Wayne Zheng: You're kind of just another guy, doing what you think is the right thing within your acceptable range.;<> Wayne Zheng: Nothing wrong with that, although our values are surely not very aligned. Wayne Zheng: If things go extremely wrong, you could be caught in a moral dilemma. I wouldn't want to see that.;<> Wayne Zheng: If I ever need your help, I'll try to avoid putting you in a difficult position. ->Thank you. Wayne Zheng: Hm? What for? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->For the trust. ->For the information. <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: It's the least I could do. Wayne Zheng: I don't think I really know you that much, though... <<#GoTo(@阿緯講評主角觀察時間。)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@頂樓事件以阿偉講評主角觀察結尾>> Wayne Zheng: That said, whether or not I need your help, I certainly want to see more allies around me. Thought: ... Thought: This conversation is getting uncomfortably serious... <<#End>> ->Those Ops people are terrible. Wayne Zheng: I'm so glad you're starting to see it!;<> Thought: This feels bad. I don't want to take sides. Wayne Zheng: There's a reason why I'm telling you about this, Jason.;<> <<#GoTo(@傾向支持阿瑋路線收尾部分)>> ->I'm not taking sides. I only want a job. Thought: That is a good answer...;<> Thought: Stay neutral. Play it safe. Don't get involved in other people's games. Wayne Zheng: Huh...;<> <<#GoTo(@說不想參與所以阿瑋表示諒解)>> ->Based what I saw, I don't think they are that bad. Wayne Zheng: What you saw?;<> Wayne Zheng: How much did you see? You were there just for one day. Thought: Ouch, that hurts...;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Time is not the point. I know what I saw. Wayne Zheng: What did they do, to have you so convinced? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->They knocked their lead out, to save a juicer! <<@阿偉糾結在算勞用詞上北宋>> Wayne Zheng: Juicer? You're using that word, too?;<> Wayne Zheng: I see they got into your head. <<#Simple(8)>> ->They punched their boss, and all you care about is a petty word? Wayne Zheng: No, you don't understand... ->What if they did get into my head? Is it important? Wayne Zheng: Have you ever thought that... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: They don't care about the language, because they don't care about how contributors feel.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->They care more than you do! ->Listen to yourself! Bullshit! Wayne Zheng: What did you say?? Thought: I shouldn't have said that... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Do you think they care about the people when they can't even put a leash on their tongues? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->While you are busy leashing your tongues, they are busy saving people. Wayne Zheng: Saving people who didn't need to get hurt in the first place! Wayne Zheng: Saving people from their own negligence! Wayne Zheng: And you call them "good people"?;<> Wayne Zheng: ... Thought: He's crushing the hell out of that coffee cup. Wayne Zheng: Looks like I can't change your mind. You believe they have done nothing wrong, huh?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not really, but certainly not as bad as you said. ->Yes. And you're biased. Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: OK. <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@阿偉嘆氣自己幹嘛那麼機掰)>> ///這裡要接到哪？////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// ->OK. I guess you're right. Wayne Zheng: You're finally getting my point. <<#End>>;//結論:一開始說支持廢場，然後因為算勞用詞被說服 ->I think you need to have thorough talk with Justin. <<#GoTo(@跟阿偉說你應該去找俊恩談看看)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->They do their work very seriously. Wayne Zheng: They are being serious for show, because you are there! <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why would they bother to impress a newbie? ->You mean, it's all fake? Wayne Zheng: Maybe not intentionally, but they are reshaping the story in their favor.;<> Wayne Zheng: To be convincing, they put up their best performance. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: They don't care about the contributors. To them, these people are just "part of the job". Wayne Zheng: Can't you tell it yet? <<#Simple(8)>> ->You're wrong. They really do care about juicers. <<#GoTo(@阿偉糾結在算勞用詞上北宋)>> ->How could you say that? Wayne Zheng: Because I have seen how they work! <<#Simple(10)>> ->What's the last time you went down there? Years ago? Thought: He's dumbstruck. Looks like I just hit the right spot. Wayne Zheng: OK, I admit, I'm hardly in touch with Ops these days.;<> Wayne Zheng: You're right. I can't base an argument on outdated knowledge... <<#GoTo(@阿偉不懂主角幹嘛一直袒護嗯嗯)>> ->How could you call what I saw "a show"? Wayne Zheng: I didn't mean it like that. I...;<> <<@阿偉不懂主角幹嘛一直袒護嗯嗯>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: I, I don't understand why you're taking their side?;<> <<@阿偉說你一直袒護操作組很問號>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm not taking sides. You're jumping to conclusions. Wayne Zheng: I spent a long time investigating. Every piece of evidence supports my conclusion.;<> Wayne Zheng: If this is what you call "jumping to conclusions", what else should I do? <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->At least have a talk with Justin Wu, one more time. <<@跟阿偉說你應該去找俊恩談看看>> //有跟吳俊恩衝突過 <<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=4||$TopFloorStiuationPath=3)>> Wayne Zheng: But you just saw what he's like!;<> Wayne Zheng: How am I supposed to talk to him? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That was because he has misunderstandings. Wayne Zheng: No. He simply doesn't want to see me.;<> ->That shouldn't stop you from trying. Wayne Zheng: I don't think so.;<> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: I do want to talk to him. I mean it.;<> Wayne Zheng: The thing is, be it formal or not, the talk always deteriorates into a fight. Wayne Zheng: I can't remember what's the last time we had a pleasant conversation…;<> Thought: So he doesn't have any personal grudge against Justin. It's all about the difference of views.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->Still, you shouldn't just give up. Wayne Zheng: I know, right? Thought: He's very frustrated. ->OK, let's forget I said that. Wayne Zheng: No, you're correct. I do agree with you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I would totally have a serious talk with him if I could.;<> Wayne Zheng: The thing is, I couldn't...;<>;//結論:支持廢場，吵架吵到後來說應該再找俊恩談談，阿偉是若同意但自己做不到 ->I still think you can do more. Wayne Zheng: What is that supposed to mean? I have done literally everything that can be done!;<> Thought: He looks extremely frustrated... Wayne Zheng: Tell me. What else can I do?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You're right. You have tried. Wayne Zheng: Thank you. Wayne Zheng: I... Wayne Zheng: Sorry, I lost control for a second.;<>;//結論:支持廢場，吵架吵到最後放棄說服主角，然後跟主角道歉 ->Did you try to establish a conversation with Justin Wu, one more time? <<#GoTo(@跟阿偉說你應該去找俊恩談看看)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->The way I see it, you're desperately looking for someone to blame. <<@跟阿偉說他根本對操作組都有偏見>> Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: OK. I admit I have attitude issues. Wayne Zheng: This is not personal. I'm only trying to solve problems created by them.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->And your solution is getting rid of them? Wayne Zheng: What? Of course not!;<> Wayne Zheng: They are critical to the operations of this farm. What gave you the idea? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You sound like you really want them gone. Wayne Zheng: I told you. This is not personal. I'm trying to solve a problem.;<> Thought: But in your mind, the person is the problem. ->Sorry. I misjudged you. Wayne Zheng: *Sigh* Not your fault. The way I said it does sound personal. I admit.;<> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> //結論:支持廢場，吵架吵到最後放棄說服主角，然後跟主角道歉 <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@阿偉嘆氣自己幹嘛那麼機掰>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: ...;<>;//演出阿緯一個大歎氣？ Wayne Zheng: I think I'm doing it wrong, Jason.;<> Wayne Zheng: You should reach your own conclusions. Wayne Zheng: And my role is to provide you with necessary information.;<> Thought: He looks sort of deflated.;<> Wayne Zheng: Sorry for trying to force biased opinions upon you.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Not a problem. I know how you feel. Wayne Zheng: Thank you. ->Trying too hard will only push me away from your side, you know? Wayne Zheng: Yes. That's why I said I was doing it wrong. Wayne Zheng: Sorry. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I hope you could consider... <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I worked at Screening for just one day, too. How do I know you're more trustworthy? Thought: He's seriously taking offense. Did I say the wrong thing...? Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: I don't need you to trust me. Trust the truth. Is it too much to ask for?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->But this "truth" comes directly from you. ->I don't have enough information to establish the truth yet. Wayne Zheng: Then go ask others, see if they all agree with me.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Others, like? Wayne Zheng: Like everyone at Screening. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Believe it or not, every single person in the office will agree with me. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Because they're all getting the "truth" straight from you. Wayne Zheng: Or what? Do you mean they should listen to the Ops people? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Exactly. Don't you want to talk this through with Justin? <<#GoTo(@跟阿偉說你應該去找俊恩談看看)>> ->I don't mean that. Still, I can't simply buy everything you say! Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: I don't need you to agree with me on everything. Wayne Zheng: My objective today is to show you the whole picture. That much I have done.;<> Wayne Zheng: As to what to make of it, you can use discretion. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Thank you for not pushing me.;//結論:支持廢場，吵架吵到最後放棄說服主角，然後跟主角道歉 Wayne Zheng: Don't. You're an independent person. Wayne Zheng: Sorry if I pushed you just now.;<> <<#GoTo(@阿偉嘆氣自己幹嘛那麼機掰)>> ->Yeah. I decide what I want to believe.;//結論:支持廢場，吵架吵到最後放棄說服主角，然後跟主角道歉 Wayne Zheng: Right. Sorry. Thought: This is awkward. Was I too blunt?;<> Thought: No. He's the one to blame here. <<#GoTo(@阿偉嘆氣自己幹嘛那麼機掰)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I want to know the other side of the story from Ops. Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Thought: I can tell he hates this idea. Better stop right now. Wayne Zheng: Go ask Justin, then. I'm sure he has fancy stories to tell.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You're very biased, man. <<#GoTo(@跟阿偉說他根本對操作組都有偏見)>> ->Why wouldn't you talk to him? <<#GoTo(@跟阿偉說你應該去找俊恩談看看)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: They'd say they are entitled to salary raises, and that salaries don't affect maintenance budget.;<> Wayne Zheng: Why are you so firmly defending them? You don't even know them. <> <<#GoTo(@阿偉說你一直袒護操作組很問號)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Actually, I think all flop farms should go. Wayne Zheng: As I said, flop farms are a necessary evil in this day and age.;<> Wayne Zheng: Without them, life will be extra hard for the unemployed. Wayne Zheng: We need to make flop farms better, more humane. Don't you think?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Flop farming is only prolonging their suffering. Wayne Zheng: True, in some cases.;<> Wayne Zheng: Will I choose to be flop contributor if I don't have any other means of living?;<> Wayne Zheng: I won't.;<> Wayne Zheng: What I decide doesn't matter. The real point here is "choose".;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Flop farms offer an option to those who want to choose them as the last straw. Wayne Zheng: What we should do is to make it a better last resort.;<> <<#GoTo(@阿偉表示可以讓算勞過好一點啊這有可能)>> ->We can't make it better, as long as flop farming is considered a profitable business. Wayne Zheng: I think you're too pessimistic. <<@阿偉表示可以讓算勞過好一點啊這有可能>> Wayne Zheng: Business operations can be both profitable and humane. This has been repeatedly proven.;<> Thought: I heard this from Fran... <> <<#Simple(10)>> ->When there is sufficient external force. Wayne Zheng: Or through internal reform. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Internal reform can be held down by the management. Wayne Zheng: I agree, but we should still give it our best try. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The best try is to shut down all flop farms, I believe. <<@阿偉放棄了覺得主角跟葉慈恩很像>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: You have a lot in common with Edith. Thought: Sounds like he doesn't approve of Edith very much... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thanks. Edith is a kind soul. Wayne Zheng: Kind soul, alright... ->What's that supposed to mean? Wayne Zheng: I mean you're alike in the pursuit of justice, fairness, and caring for the disadvantaged. Wayne Zheng: But she and I, we are not on the same path. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: OK. I have said everything I can and should say.;<> Wayne Zheng: Everyone is entitled to their own opinions. You decide whatever you choose to believe. Wayne Zheng: I...;<> <<#End>>;//結論:向葉慈恩運算產業消失最好 ->You're right. There are things we can improve. Wayne Zheng: Great! Guess I'm finally getting the message through. Wayne Zheng: Now you agree the farm should stay? <<#GoTo(@阿偉在堅持全廢路線問主角還要不要廢)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Probably. Wayne Zheng: This "probability" is exactly what I'm striving for. I'm glad you're seeing the point. <<#GoTo(@阿偉覺得說服成功所以又問一次要不要廢場)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Only in rare cases. Wayne Zheng: The existence of rare cases means it's doable. Thought: He really believes what he says... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->But you said flop farming as a business hinges on keeping the labor cost cheap. Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: I did say that, but... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't you see? You're contradicting yourself. ->You know it's but a daydream. Wayne Zheng: No, it's not. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Keeping labor cheap means no one could get rich through flop contribution.;<> Wayne Zheng: I never intended to make them rich. This is about baseline income and dignity. Wayne Zheng: Is it too much to ask for? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I agree it's actionable. <<#GoTo(@阿偉覺得說服成功所以又問一次要不要廢場)>> ->No. The nature of this business is about facilitating exploitation and crushing dignity. <<#GoTo(@阿偉放棄了覺得主角跟葉慈恩很像)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Probably. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<@阿偉覺得說服成功所以又問一次要不要廢場>> Wayne Zheng: All the more reason to keep the farm running.;<> Wayne Zheng: Now you agree with me, don't you? <> <<@阿偉在堅持全廢路線問主角還要不要廢>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I do. Silencio should stay. <<#GoTo(@同意阿偉我們不該廢廠壓)>> ->Yes, but outdated machines are still a problem. <<#GoTo(@是操作組想誤導你說機器爛爆好嗎)>> ->No. The farm should be shut down, for the greater good. <<#GoTo(@阿偉放棄了覺得主角跟葉慈恩很像)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I think Silencio should stay. <<@同意阿偉我們不該廢廠壓>> Wayne Zheng: Great!;<> Thought: He really wants to hear me say it. Better keep him happy. Thought: As long as I don't make enemies... Wayne Zheng: I know there are people who see Silencio as a form of exploitation, and wants it shut down. Wayne Zheng: I'm glad the field practice has shown you the truth.;<> Thought: Actually, Fran has been talking about this all the time... <<#Simple(10)>> ->Actually, my girlfriend talks about this all the time. Wayne Zheng: Your girlfriend? Is she in the flop farming business, too?;<> Thought: Should I tell him about Fran? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No, but she cares a lot about it. Wayne Zheng: I see. She must care a lot about social movements and civil rights.;<> Thought: Yes, but probably into it too much... ->Yeah, right here at Silencio. Thought: Oh no! Why did I say that? Wayne Zheng: Huh!;<> Wayne Zheng: Which office, if you don't mind me asking? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm not really sure. Wayne Zheng: Ah, OK.;<> Wayne Zheng: Don't blame you. We have too many offices to keep track of. Thought: He doesn't intend to pursue this subject.;<> ->She's the farm supervisor.;<> Wayne Zheng: Haha! You're funny.;<> Wayne Zheng: Wait. You mean it? For real?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No, just kidding. Wayne Zheng: For a moment I almost believed you! Haha.;<> Thought: He doesn't intend to pursue this subject. <> ->For real. Thought: He's good guy. Telling him wouldn't hurt, I guess... Wayne Zheng: I'll be damned!;<> Wayne Zheng: Then you, um... Thought: I can see some new ideas are quickly forming... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Huh? ->Are you planning something? Wayne Zheng: Ah, no. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: No, never mind.;<> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I try to keep an open mind. Wayne Zheng: I can tell. Haha. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Now that we have things clarified, let me tell you something else. Thought: He's getting serious... Wayne Zheng: There actually is a solution to those outdated machines in the Cellar. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I thought HQ doesn't want to provide the budget? Wayne Zheng: I see you have learned quite a lot about the farm. Good!;<> Wayne Zheng: HQ doesn't want to provide additional budget. <> ->You mean, they're lying to me? Wayne Zheng: Not exactly. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: HQ has a fixed annual budget for Silencio. The chunk for Ops is fixed, too.;<> Wayne Zheng: The majority of their budget is intended to keep machines fixed and serviced. Wayne Zheng: In recent years, though, that part has been steadily cut down to make way for increased salaries.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->They are expanding recruitment at Ops? Wayne Zheng: No. They only added 10 new employees in the past 5 years. ->So they need to increase the overall budget? Wayne Zheng: That is what they say. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: The root of the problem, though, is that they keep giving themselves salary raises!;<> Wayne Zheng: In the beginning it was only for the executives. Now everyone at Ops is taking a cut. Wayne Zheng: Their salaries have bumped up by 200% already, on average. How much budget do they have for everything else?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What does HQ say? Wayne Zheng: Strangely, HQ says nothing. Wayne Zheng: But maybe not. HQ did send over a new supervisor, after all.;<> //有說女朋友是督導 <<#If($ToldWeiDooDauIsGF=0)>> Wayne Zheng: I mean, your girlfriend, ha. <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: She's launching a flurry of investigations. Ops is not targeted yet.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Sounds like she's doing a great job. //有說女朋友是督導 <<#If($ToldWeiDooDauIsGF=0)>> Wayne Zheng: Says the boyfriend, ha! <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: She's really doing a great job, advocating for us at HQ. ->Why isn't Ops targeted? Wayne Zheng: Ops has access to much confidential/technological information. HQ gives them lots of privileges. Wayne Zheng: Not even the farm manager or supervisor could launch investigations against them. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->This is bad. Wayne Zheng: Very bad. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I have been trying to address this issue, but my power as the lead of Screening doesn't reach that far.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Who's the mastermind? Wayne Zheng: I thought it's their division lead. <<#Simple(6)>> ->"Thought"? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Then I realized it's a collaborative scheme. Wayne Zheng: Starting from the division lead, reaching all the way down to the bottom. They are in this together. Wayne Zheng: I suppose there won't be any change even if we manage to replace their team lead. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't you think Justin could initiate a reform from the inside? //有吵架 <<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=4||$TopFloorStiuationPath=3)>> Wayne Zheng: You saw how unreasonable he is... <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: The Justin Wu that I knew could and would push for a reform.;<> Wayne Zheng: But the man that he is right now? I don't know. Last time he told me salary raises are justified. Wayne Zheng: Money corrupts, they say. Well...;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I thought you don't have to be involved? Wayne Zheng: Don't have to, but I want to. Wayne Zheng: As they keep cutting down the maintenance budget, more contributors will get hurt or killed. Wayne Zheng: I genuinely care about the contributors. It's not my responsibility, but I want to do something. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: That was a lot said. The key part is farm closure.;<> Wayne Zheng: They are lobbying HQ, and we must stop them. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What do they get out of farm closure? Wayne Zheng: Nothing. They just want an exit plan before it's too late. ->Justin said they don't want to see more people hurt. Wayne Zheng: Shameless lie...;<> Wayne Zheng: Who's hurting those people? The Ops team! Wayne Zheng: He's fabricating a story to cover their real goal.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: The Cellar is getting worse by the day. Soon, they won't be able to hide it anymore. Wayne Zheng: What happened yesterday and today are the beginning of the end...;<> Wayne Zheng: Eventually they will screw up big time, and heads will roll. Wayne Zheng: They are trying to shut down the farm before the day of reckoning. <<#Simple(8)>> ->This is terrible. Wayne Zheng: People lose their sense of right and wrong one step at a time. I suppose that's what happened to Ops.;<> Wayne Zheng: It started with an innocent salary raise. Eventually... <> ->Won't they lose their jobs? Wayne Zheng: No. Vision spent a lot of money training these engineers and technicians.;<> Wayne Zheng: They will be transferred to other farms upon the closure of Silencio. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see why they want to shut it down now. Wayne Zheng: Yeah. ->Yeah. Thought: He seems to be struggling over something. Wayne Zheng: We at Screening, though, are a different story. Most of us will lose the job. Thought: So his effort to save Silencio has a personal factor in it... ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Now you see. You're joining Silencio at a pivotal time.;<> <<#Simple(12)>> ->Nothing to pivot on, if it's shut down. Wayne Zheng: Hey, don't be so pessimistic!;<> Wayne Zheng: The reason why we are having this long conversation is to prevent farm closure! <> ->I don't know if I'm hired yet. Wayne Zheng: True, hahaha! //判定錄取 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount<2 )>> Wayne Zheng: Show some more confidence, man!;<> Wayne Zheng: Ms. Yeh likes you. The job is pretty much yours. <> <<#EndIf>> //判定不錄取 <<#If($InterviewHandleBadCount>1 )>> Wayne Zheng: Ms. Yeh wasn't very impressed yesterday. <<#EndIf>> ->Hope everything works out in the end. Wayne Zheng: It will. It has to. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: OK. I think this is it... <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>>

title: 頂樓俊恩路線
body:
//我找你來是想告訴妳我已經決底不錄取妳了。
//以妳在操作組的表現相信篩選組一定會錄取你。
//希望你可以以今天的經驗告訴篩選組的大家操作組的情況。


Justin Wu: Oh, hi!;<<PlayUntil(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>


Justin Wu: ...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
Justin Wu: You really turned Wayne down?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Yeah.;<<PlayUntil(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_頂樓左看)>>
->Huh?;<<PlayUntil(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_頂樓左看)>>
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: Um...
Justin Wu: Did he say anything when you told him you're meeting up with me?;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
//如果有告訴鄭緯
<<#If($ToldWeiWuInvited=true)>>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Nope. Nothing.
        Justin Wu: I see. Good.
    ->He was a bit frustrated, I think.
        Justin Wu: Yeah.
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>
//如果沒告訴鄭緯
<<#If($ToldWeiWuInvited=false)>>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->I didn't tell him about you.
        Justin Wu: I see.
        Thought: I can't tell if he's relieved or disappointed...
    ->Why ask?
        Justin Wu: Just wondering.
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>

Justin Wu: ...
Justin Wu: ...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
Thought: He seems...a bit down?
Justin Wu: Say...
Justin Wu: This is my quiet spot. Every time I need to cool down, I come up here.
Justin Wu: I guess Wayne has the same habit.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->How do you know?
    Thought: That chuckle feels sad.
    Justin Wu: Because we used to come here together.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: We joined Silencio at about the same time, both working under Screening.
Justin Wu: My situation back then has a lot in common with Cathy Hsieh.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->System engineer?
    Justin Wu: Yes, although I was much less experienced.
    Justin Wu: She's way better than I was.
->How did you end up in Ops?
    Justin Wu: It happened a couple of years down the road.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: Those were interesting times. Ms. Yeh just became the manager not long ago.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
Justin Wu: A lot of changes were happening, people transferred around.
Justin Wu: Between Wayne and I, we worked pretty closely.
Justin Wu: Sometimes there's competition, but overall we saw each other as comrades.
<<#Simple(6)>>
->But not anymore?
    Justin Wu: Anymore what?;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
    Justin Wu: Comrades?
    <<PlayUntil(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: Probably because of the transfer. Different positions, different perspectives.
Justin Wu: I saw the underbelly of this farm when I got transferred to Ops.
<<#Simple(6)>>
->Why did you go to Ops, to begin with?
    Justin Wu: ...
    Justin Wu: Why? What do you think?;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
    Thought: Why ask me...?
    <<@猜離開篩選組原因跟俊恩聊的時候>>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Because they pay better? ($GuessPayHigherFilter);<<Set($GuessEnLeaveReason,$GuessEnLeaveReason+1)>>
        <<Set($GuessPayHigherFilter,-1)>>
        Thought: Looks like he approves.
        Justin Wu: It's one reason.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
        Justin Wu: They pay technicians and engineers more, alright.
        Justin Wu: But that was only secondary.
    ->Transferred against your will? ($GuessForceLeaveFilter);<<Set($GuessEnLeaveReason,$GuessEnLeaveReason+1)>>
        <<Set($GuessForceLeaveFilter,-1)>>
        Justin Wu: Nah, no such thing.
        Justin Wu: I actually had a fight with Ms. Yeh to make this transfer happen.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
        Justin Wu: She didn't want me to switch over to Ops. In the end she agreed it was a great idea, though.
        <<#Simple(6)>>
        ->How so?
            Justin Wu: Later she realized she needed someone trustworthy to keep Ops under watch.
            Thought: Trustworthy?
            Thought: As if she had no power over Ops?
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
    ->To avoid someone you don't like at Screening? ($GuessHateFilterSomeone);<<Set($GuessEnLeaveReason,$GuessEnLeaveReason+1)>>
        <<Set($GuessHateFilterSomeone,-1)>>
        Justin Wu: Wow, you're perceptive.
        Justin Wu: Very close.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
        Justin Wu: I seldom "don't like" someone.
        Justin Wu: Or maybe I simply dislike all people equally?
        Thought: ...?????
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->What does that mean?
        ->Meaning you don't like me, either?
            Justin Wu: No, sorry, don't get me wrong.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
            Justin Wu: I'm kinda nitpicky. There are a lot of things that I don't like.
            Justin Wu: Knowing it's not a popular trait, I generally avoid hanging out with people.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Justin Wu: That said, there wasn't anyone who I particularly wanted to avoid.
    ->Just tell me already. ($JustTellMeLeaveReason)
        Justin Wu: OK.
        <<#GoTo(@猜完說明離開原因囉)>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
    //三個理由都猜過就主動說明
    <<#If($GuessEnLeaveReason < 3)>>
        Justin Wu: Wanna try again?;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
        //猜過一次以後就開啟不繼續猜的選項
        <<#If($GuessEnLeaveReason > 0)>>
            <<Set($JustTellMeLeaveReason,1)>>
        <<#EndIf>>
        <<#GoTo(@猜離開篩選組原因跟俊恩聊的時候)>>
    <<#EndIf>>
    <<@猜完說明離開原因囉>>
    Justin Wu: I transferred to Ops because of Wayne.
    <<@解釋為啥去操作組>>
    Justin Wu: The lead of Screening – the one back then – was getting a promotion. Ms. Yeh needed someone to fill the gap. It was either Wayne or me.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->You fought over the opportunity?
    ->He ousted you?
        Justin Wu: Nope. The opposite.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Justin Wu: I asked to be transferred to Ops, to get out of the competition.
    Justin Wu: He tried so hard to get the position. I thought I'd better leave the opportunity to him.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->He must be happy for what you did.
        Justin Wu: Well, that's what I thought...
    ->But he's offended?;<<Set($GuessAWeiReactionAtTheTime,true)>>
        Justin Wu: ...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
        Thought: He's surprised.
        Justin Wu: ...You know him better than I did.
        Justin Wu: You're right.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Justin Wu: He came to me, fuming.
    Justin Wu: He said I was rigging the game, made him look like a cheat.
    Justin Wu: I had no idea what he was so angry about, but somehow just couldn't find anything to say.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓低頭)>>
    Justin Wu: Perhaps I was giving myself an excuse to quit, in case I do go against him and lose.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_頂樓左看返回)>>
    Justin Wu: Anyway, I told him the competition was still on, albeit with different rules.
    Justin Wu: We both thought Ops were a bunch of assholes, right? So I was gonna change them.
    Justin Wu: Let's see who's the better leader when I become the head of Ops, I said.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓低頭返回)>>
    Justin Wu: We made a promise. We were going to shape the future, together.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_頂樓左看)>>
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->Then how did you drift apart?
        Justin Wu: ...
        Justin Wu: He stopped trusting me.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
        Justin Wu: One thing I learned, once transferred to Ops, is that everything happens for a reason.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_頂樓左看返回)>>
        Justin Wu: I tried to explain to Wayne why Ops did what they did. He didn't believe, though. Said I become one of them.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
        Justin Wu: I never thought he could be so unreasonable. He—
        Justin Wu: ...;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_頂樓左看)>>
        Justin Wu: Well, that's as much as you need to know.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓低頭)>>
        Justin Wu: I don't want to speak ill of them. Wayne is a good man, an outstanding man.
        Justin Wu: It's just that...
        Justin Wu: I wish he could see things in a more rational way.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓低頭返回)>>
        //俊恩反擊啊偉說他待過篩選組，到是他沒待過操作組，阿緯反擊他帶過但沒當過面談元。不夠瞭解當地人民的需求。也沒當過組長。
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Justin Wu: That's probably more than you wanted to know, sorry...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
    Justin Wu: Couldn't help myself...
    Justin Wu: Maybe because I'm seeing my old self in you.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->What exactly reminded you of your old self?
        Justin Wu: I don't know. Just a general feeling...
    ->No problem at all. I really appreciate the information.
        Justin Wu: Thank you.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Justin Wu: Let's cut to the chase.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
    Justin Wu: I want to know what you think about the farm.
    <<#GoTo(@俊恩開始說來頂樓是要講啥咪)>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: Screening and Ops are very different. Worlds apart, you could say.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
Justin Wu: The environment, the responsibilities, the team, everything.
Justin Wu: Screening deals with people, all about caring and improvising.
Justin Wu: Ops, though, works with machines. We follow rules to the letter.
Justin Wu: If I could choose again, though, I'd probably still choose Ops.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
<<#GoTo(@解釋為啥去操作組)>>



<<@俊恩開始說來頂樓是要講啥咪>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->I thought this is only a casual talk?
    Justin Wu: No. Sorry I was wandering off.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
    Thought: He's apologetic.
    <<PlayUntil(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
->What do you want to talk about?
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: I want to talk about the possibility of shutting down this farm.
Justin Wu: It would be great to approach this issue from a fresh perspective, as someone with no stake in it.
Justin Wu: What do you think? Should Silencio be shut down?;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->I don't know enough about Silencio to form an opinion yet.
    Justin Wu: Good.
    Justin Wu: Good thinking for a non-professional.
    Thought: Non-professional, huh…
    Justin Wu: Don't be so eager to form opinions about things you don't understand.
->It should be shut down.
    Justin Wu: Why?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Because you guys keep it running, and you say so.
        Justin Wu: Good.
        Justin Wu: Good thinking for a non-professional.
        Thought: Non-professional, huh…
        Justin Wu: When you don't understand something, listen to the professionals.
    ->Because the Cellar is seriously beyond help.
    ->Someone died! Isn't that a good enough reason?
        Justin Wu: Yeah. Even a non-professional can tell how bad it has become.
        Thought: Non-professional, huh…
    <<#EndSimple>>
->I don't think it really has to go.
    Justin Wu: Why's that?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->You can still ask HQ for resources to fix the Cellar.
    ->It's hurting some people, but helping far more.
    ->Not sure, just feeling there must be other options.
        Justin Wu: A feeling?
        Thought: He's doubting me.
        Justin Wu: How could you chalk something this serious up to a feeling?
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->A feeling is all I have, man.
        ->I don't know enough to have an informed opinion yet. Have to trust feelings.
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Justin Wu: Then you shouldn't have said anything.
        Justin Wu: Don't be so eager to form opinions about things you don't understand.
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->You were asking specifically for my opinion, remember?
            Justin Wu: You could have said you don't have one.
            Thought: He's not wrong, yet...
        ->You're right.
        <<#EndSimple>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: The problem with Silencio is that they are listening too much to the non-professionals.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看低頭)>>
Justin Wu: HQ will be calling a meeting about Silencio's fate soon.
Justin Wu: I hope they will find an agreement on farm closure this time.
Justin Wu: However, Wayne and Screening will push against the idea with all they have.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看低頭返回)>>


//if沒錄取操作組
<<#If($UpShelfWrongCount>3)>>
    Justin Wu: I don't intend to recruit you into Ops, Jason. You may end up with Screening.
    Justin Wu: Still, I want you to see the actual situation.
<<#EndIf>>


//if有錄取操作組
<<#If($UpShelfWrongCount<4)>>
    Justin Wu: I want to welcome you to Ops, Jason, although chances are you will choose Screening in the end.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Probably.
        //
    ->What made you think that?
        Justin Wu: Because the Cellar is a terrible place. I understand most people wouldn't want to work here.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Justin Wu: Before you make your decision, I must make sure you see the truth.
<<#EndIf>>

Justin Wu: They are wrong. Keeping Silencio running is wrong.
Justin Wu: I hope you understand that, even if you decide to join Screening.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->I don't agree with—
    Justin Wu: It's alright.
    Thought: Let me finish, dude.
    Justin Wu: I'm not trying to convince you.
->I will keep that in mind.
    Justin Wu: Good.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: All this time working at Silencio, I have learned one thing.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看返回)>>
Justin Wu: People won't believe or accept things that they don't understand.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_頂樓左看返回)>>
Justin Wu: I've given up trying to convince them. No use.
Justin Wu: All I need to do is to grasp the leadership and make the right decisions for them.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_頂樓左看)>>
Justin Wu: This is the only way forward.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_頂樓右看)>>
<<#Simple(12)>>
->Good thinking.
    Justin Wu: Yeah.
->I think that is only for the last resort.
    Justin Wu: Silencio is a behemoth that holds hundreds of thousands, both juicers and workers.
    Justin Wu: To prevent it from doing more harm? I think I'm desperate enough for the last resort.
    Thought: He's dead serious about it.
->I don't agree with you.
    Justin Wu: And I don't need your agreement.
    Justin Wu: Like I said, if I have the leadership, your opinion doesn't matter.
    Justin Wu: I will make the right decision for everyone, you included.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->That is cruel.
        Justin Wu: Cruel? Bypassing the bickering to have an urgent issue addressed asap?
        Justin Wu: Wayne's Mr. Nice Guy approach is crueler, from my point of view.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<#End>>

title: 回辦公室看到新聞
body:
//進到篩選辦公室大家都在看新聞，玩家往左走球球會拉住主角

Quentin Qiu: Hey, Jayster, welcome back!;<< Play (邱奕宏, MBU_邱奕宏_側坐後躺轉頭返回) >>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->What's on the news?;<<Set($DLState,瞬到球球旁邊看電視)>>
    Quentin Qiu: Someone fell from the HQ building, or something.
    Quentin Qiu: It's on the TV. There.;<< Play (邱奕宏, MBU_邱奕宏_側坐後躺轉頭) >>
->Yeah, need to clock out.;<<Set($DLState,瞬到球球旁邊看電視)>>
    Quentin Qiu: Did you check the news?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->On the TV right now?
        Quentin Qiu: Yeah.
    ->What news?
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Quentin Qiu: Someone fell from the HQ building, or something.
    Quentin Qiu: It's on the TV. There.;<< Play (邱奕宏, MBU_邱奕宏_側坐後躺轉頭) >>
<<#EndSimple>>
<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站看新聞) >>

//攝影機拉近螢幕，看得到記者在播報，電視開始跳對話框
;<<Set($DLState,攝影機慢慢拉進程芳墜樓新聞)>>
TV: Breaking news from Vision Corporation headquarters, where a dramatic incident has just unfolded.
TV: A woman in a white coat plunged several stories into the building's atrium, shocking onlookers.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站看新聞激動1) >>;//glitch加強，心跳
TV: Eyewitnesses estimate she fell from the third floor, landing amidst a horrified crowd.
TV: The woman was critically injured and unresponsive at the scene.
TV: Emergency services quickly deployed a drone to transport her to the hospital, where her condition remains critical.
TV: The incident has sparked widespread speculation, with police investigations ongoing.
TV: Security personnel at the site identified the woman as a senior executive of Vision.
Quentin Qiu: Huh? Jason? Are you OK?;<< Play (邱奕宏, MBU_邱奕宏_側坐後躺轉頭返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站看新聞激動2) >>;//glitch加強，心跳
TV: The location of the incident adds an element of mystery to the case.
Quentin Qiu: H-hey, why are you so pale? Are you alright?
TV: As to—
TV: ...;//主播摸耳機低頭好像聽到新訊息
TV: We've just received an update on the identity of the woman involved in the fall.
TV: She has been identified as Ms. Cheng, a senior executive of Vision Corporation.;<<Set($DLState,戴士傑看新聞解控)>>
//畫面glitch到爆，心跳，黑掉
//黑畫面過程中聽到碰撞聲、救護車的聲音、車子喇叭、醫院人群

title: 病房裡
body:
//畫面亮起，還有點模糊，主角站在程芳病房前，突然頭痛的感覺，一名護士緊張得站在後面。
//病房或門口可以調查病例

Thought: Fran...
Thought: Thank gods...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_便服3_側站低頭) >>
Thought: Fran is alright, at least not—
Thought: Ugh...
Thought: My head. It's happening again...
<<Set($DLState,護士叫主角離開病房開始)>>
Mia Lai: Um, excuse me...?
Mia Lai: Excuse me, sir!;<< Play (賴美雲, MBU_賴美雲_側站插腰) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_便服3_側站低頭返回) >>
Mia Lai: Sir, would you please wait outside?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_便服3_側站看外) >>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Huh? Why?
    Mia Lai: Ms. Cheng's condition is stabilized, but the doctor needs to run some further examinations.
    Mia Lai: We'd appreciate if you could wait outside.
->Where am I?
    Mia Lai: Um, this is Harmony Medical Center.
    Thought: Why would I even ask? I can tell it's a shabby hospital.
    Thought: I must transfer Fran to a better place. She—
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
//一個看起來很粗勇的醫生走進來，可以看到手臂有刺青
<<Set($DLState,司徒假醫生進病房)>>
Mia Lai: Sir, please—;<< Play (賴美雲, MBU_賴美雲_側站插腰返回) >>
Nin Situ: Get ready for examinations. How are her vitals?
Mia Lai: Um, excuse me, you're...?;<< Play (賴美雲, MBU_賴美雲_側站微慌) >>
Nin Situ: Her attending physician. Didn't you read her records?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_便服3_側站看外返回) >>
Nin Situ: Go, get everything ready.;<< Play (賴美雲, MBU_賴美雲_側站微慌返回) >>
Mia Lai: Sure, right away!
<<#End>>
//護士小跑離開，醫生走去用機器監看程芳的狀況

title: 到站以後
body: Michael Hsiao: Hi! Michael Hsiao: Morning, Mr. Dai! Thought: Morning? I'm pretty sure it's evening... Thought: He made the same mistake yesterday. Michael Hsiao: Ouch, sorry! I mean, evening!;<< PlayUntil (蕭勇明, MBU_蕭勇明_側站抓頭) >> Michael Hsiao: I used to work morning shifts. Haven't fully adjusted yet, haha...;<< Play (蕭勇明, MBU_蕭勇明_側站抓頭返回) >> <<#Simple(8)>> ->No biggie. I've just started working night shifts, too. Michael Hsiao: Ha! Michael Hsiao: Gonna work hard!;<< Play (蕭勇明, MBU_蕭勇明_側站比讚) >> Thought: He looks at me expectantly, as if waiting for some uplifting response...? <<#Simple(6)>> ->Yeah! Right! Keep up the good work! Michael Hsiao: Yay! //進入擊掌小遊戲。沒按會尷尬 <<#GoTo(@配合蕭勇明的嗨)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Michael Hsiao: Uh, um... Michael Hsiao: You have a productive day, too! <<@配合蕭勇明的嗨>> Thought: I can't deal with pathologically upbeat people like this one...;<< Play (蕭勇明, MBU_蕭勇明_側站比讚返回) >> Thought: Can't deal with people like him... ->Show some professionalism!;<> Michael Hsiao: Y-yes! You're right!;<< PlayUntil (蕭勇明, MBU_蕭勇明_側站點頭) >> Michael Hsiao: Will definitely pay more attention! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Michael Hsiao: Hey, if you don't mind me asking... Michael Hsiao: Your position, is it screening consultant?;<< Play (蕭勇明, MBU_蕭勇明_側站插腰) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm not sure yet. ->More or less. Michael Hsiao: Ah, I see! Michael Hsiao: Sorry if I'm too nosy. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why ask? <<#GoTo(@問蕭勇明幹嘛問我是不是面談員)>> ->Any other questions? Michael Hsiao: No. That's it! ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Why ask? <<@問蕭勇明幹嘛問我是不是面談員>> Michael Hsiao: Actually... I need to ask someone in the company for a favor. Michael Hsiao: But I don't know, not sure yet. Michael Hsiao: Thought I should make a list of everyone I know. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Do we know each other? Michael Hsiao: Of course! We did a proper introduction! //如果之前有選之前認識 <<#If($HowToIntroduceYourself>0)>>;//第一日第一次跟蕭永明見面有主動說出他的名字 Michael Hsiao: And we did meet before, although I forgot about it all. Sorry. Michael Hsiao: This time I definitely won't forget you! <<#EndIf>> ->I see. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Why is that any of your business?;<> Michael Hsiao: Uh... Michael Hsiao: I'm just curious... <<#EndSimple>> <> Amelia Kuo: Mike! Enough fooling around. Come here!;<> Michael Hsiao: Coming!;<< Play (蕭勇明, MBU_蕭勇明_側站插腰返回) >> Michael Hsiao: Won't keep you any longer.;<> Michael Hsiao: Have a productive day!;<< PlayUntil (蕭勇明, MBU_蕭勇明_側站點頭) >> <<#End>> //蕭勇明

title: 主角想一下今天要幹嘛
body:
Thought: Um, I forgot to ask yesterday...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_便服2_側站抬頭) >>
Thought: Whom should I report to, now?
Thought: Guess I should check with Wayne or Ms. Yeh. Either way, better put the uniform on.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_便服2_側站抬頭返回) >>
<<#End>>

title: 跟俊恩等地窖電梯
body: <<#If($DLState=比俊恩早跑到地窖電梯)>> Justin Wu: I see you're eager for action.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why's that? Justin Wu: Because you're trotting. Thought: Well, he's right... ->More or less. Justin Wu: I see. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#EndIf>> Justin Wu: These high-speed elevators are the only way down to the Cellar. <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Can't we take other elevators? Justin Wu: It's a very long descent. By regular elevators, the ride will take forever.;<> Justin Wu: Plus, regular products won't survive the temperature and humidity down there. Justin Wu: Hence these rugged, hyper-fast elevators.;<> ->Acknowledged. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Too long a ride and not enough elevators. We may have to wait for a long while. Justin Wu: Ops and Logistics share these three elevators, you know.;<> Justin Wu: Ever wondered why we seldom come up here? This is the reason. Justin Wu: We can't deal with the wait, except for at the beginning and end of a shift.;<> Justin Wu: As a result, these elevators basically belong to Logistics.;<> Justin Wu: They maintain and service all facilities within Silencio, including the Cellar. Thought: I didn't ask for so much information... <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->All facilities? Justin Wu: Right. So far, you have only seen the surface of Silencio. ->I thought below the surface there's only the Cellar? Justin Wu: Understandable, but that is not true. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: A flop farm runs on heavy machinery and many systems.;<> Justin Wu: Most of them are buried deep down below. The Cellar is only one of them.;<> Thought: He is the boring kind, alright... Thought: But again, I feel more at home with people like him.;<> Justin Wu: ...;<> Justin Wu: The elevator won't arrive any time soon. Anything else you want to know?;<> //ElevatorJunEnAskCount //跟俊恩等電梯時使用預設為-1，玩家每問一個問題+2 <<@跟俊恩等電梯問問題時間>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What is the Cellar like? ($AskCellarWhatLook) <> <> Justin Wu: Simply put, it's HUGE.;<> Thought: Wha...? <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Wha...? ->Can you be, like, more specific? Justin Wu: Hmm... Justin Wu: It's dark. Cold. Metal surfaces everywhere. Thought: ... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Ah. I see. Thank you. Justin Wu: You're welcome. This guided tour is part of my job. ->That is...abstract. Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Sorry, I'm not good at explaining things.;<> Thought: He seems genuinely apologetic... Justin Wu: I failed my job.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Relax. It's not a problem at all. Justin Wu: Thank you. Thought: Seems like he really appreciates. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Cathy Hsieh comes over to Ops all the time.
