Thought: It's not a bad thing, though. <<#EndIf>> George Liao: Really? George Liao: You mean, they have left the farm? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Don't think so. It's just that I couldn't locate them. George Liao: Same thing. ->They could be in places where I don't have access to. George Liao: Impossible. <<#EndSimple>> George Liao: Cynthia, maybe. Jade has the same security clearance as yours.;<> George Liao: If you checked everywhere, at least one of them must be there. George Liao: Or you're lying.;<> <<@廖哥報氣說明林靜慧搞砸了啥欸>> Thought: He's very, very angry now... George Liao: You know what this is about? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->About Jade's hard drive. <<#If($SaidToldLinGinHueLiouFind=false)>> George Liao: So, you did find them. <<#EndIf>> <<#If($SaidToldLinGinHueLiouFind=true)>> George Liao: They told you that? <<#EndIf>> George Liao: Then you decided to buy them some time, huh? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. ->Yes, but they will find it very soon. <<#EndSimple>> George Liao: Shut up.;<> Thought: ... George Liao: The hard drive is a whole hour overdue.;<> <<#GoTo(@廖哥飆問主角知不知道多嚴重蝦)>> ->No, I don't. <<#EndSimple>> George Liao: Jade Lin has a hard drive, containing confidential document for HQ. George Liao: This drive should have been on my desk an hour ago. <<@廖哥飆問主角知不知道多嚴重蝦>> George Liao: Do you have any idea what the consequences are? George Liao: HQ is chasing me. They could send people any time...;<> George Liao: ... George Liao: Good. Now I have to tell them Jade lost it. George Liao: I don't want this to happen, but she could be fired.;<> George Liao: Not my fault. She's been making one mistake after another. Incompetence is not to be tolerated. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Acknowledged. // ->Can't you wait for her a little bit more? George Liao: And risk my own job?;<> George Liao: Right now I'm already held partially responsible. George Liao: Collaborating with her will only make my case worse.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->But she really needs this job.;<> George Liao: ... George Liao: Who doesn't? ->Maybe she's already found it. Maybe she's on her way here now.;<> George Liao: ... George Liao: No. I must report to HQ right away. Damage control. <<#GoTo(@跳過變數設定，確保是有幫說話路線)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<@跳過變數設定，確保是有幫說話路線>> George Liao: Here is how it goes. I will report to HQ. George Liao: And you are dismissed. Thank you.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Acknowledged. // ->You really won't help her? George Liao: I said you're dismissed. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: I guess this is as much as I can do...;<> <<#End>> ->Jade lost the hard drive. //if當初說會幫忙拖延 <<#If($LieYouDontFindLin=1)>> Thought: Hey! I promised her to buy some time! <<#EndIf>> <<@告訴廖哥林靜慧姊姊弄丟硬碟欸>> George Liao: What!?;<> George Liao: HQ is demanding that drive! George Liao: What the hell is she doing now?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->They are looking for the hard drive. ->I don't know. <<#EndSimple>> George Liao: The hard drive is a whole hour overdue. <<#GoTo(@廖哥飆問主角知不知道多嚴重蝦)>> ->I told them that you're looking for them.;<> //if當初說會幫忙拖延 <<#If($AskWhereIsHeShinYeeInpolite=1)>> Thought: Hey! I promised her to buy some time! <<#EndIf>> George Liao: What did they say? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Jade said she lost the hard drive. <<#GoTo(@告訴廖哥林靜慧姊姊弄丟硬碟欸)>> ->They didn't say anything in particular. <<#GoTo(@廖哥報氣說明林靜慧搞砸了啥欸)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>>

title: 主動去找蕭勇明
body:
//蕭勇明在滑手機或是看飲料機，總之某種特殊待機


//if沒有第二日開場罵了蕭永明兩次
<<#If($AttitudeTowardShou=-2)>>
    Michael Hsiao: Um, Mr. Dai, hello.
    Thought: ...Somehow I feel he doesn't want to talk to me?
    Michael Hsiao: I have work to do! S-sorry!
    <<Set($DLState,蕭勇明一個怕先跑)>>
    <<#Simple(3)>>
    ->Wait.;<<Set($DLState,蕭勇明一個怕先跑後被主角叫住)>>
        Michael Hsiao: Huh? What?;<<Play(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_側站擔心)>>
        Michael Hsiao: I know you don't like me very much, so...
        <<#Simple(6)>>
        ->Who said I don't like you?
            Michael Hsiao: Eh?
            Michael Hsiao: Phew... Good to know.;<<Play(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_側站擔心返回)>>
            Michael Hsiao: I thought I must have offended you somehow, haha!
            Thought: Truth is, he did sort of offend me...
            <<#GoTo(@蕭勇明以為你不爽他一個改觀)>>
        ->I need to talk to you.
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Michael Hsiao: My break time is running out. Can I do something for you?;<<PlayUntil(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_側站擔心返回)>>
        <<#GoTo(@問蕭永明那個硬碟跑去哪啦)>>
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Thought: What a waste of time.
    <<Set($DLState,蕭勇明去拿硬碟_兇他又放跑)>>
<<#EndIf>>


//if沒有第二日開場沒有罵蕭永明兩次
<<#If($AttitudeTowardShou>-2)>>
    Michael Hsiao: Hey, Mr. Dai!
    Michael Hsiao: How are you today?
    <<@蕭勇明以為你不爽他一個改觀>>
    Michael Hsiao: Oh, I don't have too much time.;<<Play(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_比七)>>
    Michael Hsiao: I'm taking a short break. It's almost patrol time again.
    Michael Hsiao: Can I help you?;<<PlayUntil(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_比七返回)>>
<<#EndIf>>

<<@問蕭永明那個硬碟跑去哪啦>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Did you search the corridor outside the contract rooms yesterday?
    <<Set($TalkToShawAboutHardDrive,0)>>
    Michael Hsiao: That's the police's job.;<<Play(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_側站插腰)>>
    Thought: He's right...
    Michael Hsiao: We did have a quick look around before they arrived, though.
    Michael Hsiao: Why ask?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Did you find anything there?
        Michael Hsiao: We did.
        Michael Hsiao: There was a hard drive.;<<PlayUntil(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_側站插腰低頭)>>
    ->Did you happen to find a hard drive there?
        Michael Hsiao: We did.
        <<PlayUntil(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_側站插腰低頭)>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Michael Hsiao: There's HQ's label on it. My boss will transfer it to lost & found later.
    Michael Hsiao: Is it yours?;<<Play(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_側站插腰低頭返回)>>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Yes.
        Michael Hsiao: Ah. You confirm it's yours?;<<Play(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_側站插腰返回)>>
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->Of course. Please go fetch it.
            Michael Hsiao: Sure!
        ->Okay, it's a friend of mine's.
            Michael Hsiao: Ah, I see!
            <<#GoTo(@跟蕭勇明說弄丟硬碟名字是)>>
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Michael Hsiao:  I'll get it for you.;<<Set($DLState,蕭勇明去拿硬碟_說是自己)>>
    ->Actually, a colleague of mine dropped it.
        <<Play(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_側站插腰返回)>>
        <<@跟蕭勇明說弄丟硬碟名字是>>
        Michael Hsiao: What's the colleague's name?
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->Cynthia Ho.;<<Set($DLState,蕭勇明去拿硬碟_說錯名字)>>
        ->Cynthia Lin.;<<Set($DLState,蕭勇明去拿硬碟_說錯名字)>>
        ->Jade Lin.;<<Set($DLState,蕭勇明去拿硬碟_說對名字)>>
        ->Jade Ho.;<<Set($DLState,蕭勇明去拿硬碟_說錯名字)>>
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Michael Hsiao: Let me verify a few things and fetch it for you.
        <<#End>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
->Work first. Don't let me keep you.
    Michael Hsiao: Ah, sure.;<<Play(蕭勇明,MBU_蕭勇明_側站點頭)>>
    Michael Hsiao: Bye.
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->Bye.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    <<Set($DLState,蕭勇明去拿硬碟_直接放棄)>>
    Thought: ...What am I doing?
    Thought: Didn't I come here to get the hard drive?
    <<#End>>
<<#EndSimple>>

title: 路人事件:評論昨天的事件

title: 郭悅美把硬碟拿出來
body:
//郭悅美走過來
Amelia Kuo: Hey!;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭返回)>>
Amelia Kuo: Jason Dai?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->You're?
    Amelia Kuo: Amelia Kuo, leader of this security squad.
->Yes, it's me.
<<#EndSimple>>

<<#If($DLState=蕭勇明去拿硬碟_說錯名字||$DLState=蕭勇明去拿硬碟_說是自己)>>
    Amelia Kuo: I'm afraid I can't give you the hard drive.
    <<#If($DLState=蕭勇明去拿硬碟_說錯名字)>>
        Amelia Kuo: The owner's name you gave was wrong.
    <<#EndIf>>
    <<#If($DLState=蕭勇明去拿硬碟_說是自己)>>
        Amelia Kuo: And this hard drive is not registered to you.
    <<#EndIf>>
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->My bad. This time I'll get it right.
        Amelia Kuo: No.
        Amelia Kuo: According to company policies, you have only one chance.
        Amelia Kuo: Now we must keep this hard drive until my boss gives the green light.
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->What do you mean?
            Amelia Kuo: I mean, you won't be able to claim this hard drive today.
        ->Please. Do me a favor.
            Amelia Kuo: No. Policy is policy.
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
    ->Okay.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Amelia Kuo: One more thing that you may want to know.;<<Play(郭悅美,MBU_郭悅美_側站插腰手指)>>
    Amelia Kuo: Since you gave the wrong name, now this hard drive can't be claimed even by its registered owner.
    Amelia Kuo: We can't give it to anyone without the approval from my superior.
    Amelia Kuo: Now, please leave.;<<Play(郭悅美,MBU_郭悅美_側站插腰手指返回)>>
    <<#Simple(10)>>
    ->I want to talk to Michael Hsiao.
        Amelia Kuo: No need. I'm his superior.
        Amelia Kuo: Now, please leave.
    ->Somebody's job depends on this!
        Amelia Kuo: I don't care.
        Amelia Kuo: Now, please leave.
    ->Come on! Be reasonable!
        Amelia Kuo: Now.
        Amelia Kuo: Please.
        Amelia Kuo: Leave.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    <<PlayUntil(郭悅美,MBU_郭悅美_側站插腰返回)>>
    <<Set($DLState,郭悅美拒絕主角後回家)>>
    Thought: Damn, a bunch of time wasted...
    Thought: Guess I have to tell George Liao, then.
    <<#End>>
<<#EndIf>>

<<#If($DLState=蕭勇明去拿硬碟_說對名字)>>
    Amelia Kuo: Here is your hard drive.;<<Play(郭悅美,MBU_郭悅美_側站插腰給硬碟)>>;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站拿硬碟)>>
    Amelia Kuo: Don't drop it again.
    <<PlayUntil(郭悅美,MBU_郭悅美_側站插腰給硬碟返回)>>
    <<Set($DLState,郭悅美認證主角後回家)>>
    Thought: Yes! Found it.;<<Set($FoundLinGinHueDisk,true)>>;<<PlayUntil(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站拿硬碟返回)>>
    <<#End>>
<<#EndIf>>

title: 雙流氓質問主角跟程芳關係
body:
Tighe Liu: This is the guy?
Nathan Chuang: Yes. Leave the talking to me.
Nathan Chuang: Evening, sir. May I please know your name?;<< Play (莊智宣, MBU_莊智宣_側站插腰) >>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Jason Dai.
    Tighe Liu: Never heard of.

    Tighe Liu: Ah?
->None of your business.
->Who the hell are you people?
    Nathan Chuang: I'd very much appreciate your cooperation, sir.
<<#EndSimple>>

Nathan Chuang: Now, what is the relationship between you and Ms. Frances Cheng?

    Nathan Chuang: Ah, Mr. Boyfriend.
->You know Fran?

Tighe Liu: Easy. Let's beat the answers out of him!;<<Set($DLState,劉承虎打架前置鏡頭拉近)>>;<< PlayUntil (劉承虎, MBU_劉承虎_激動轉打架) >>

Tighe Liu: This won't take long.;<<Set($DLState,莊先生無奈走掉醫院)>>
<<#End>>

Thought: Is Justin sighing...?
<<#End>>

body: Jesse Lai: Missy Kaylee has been stuck on the same error for a full week, you know that? Jesse Lai: I rewrote her code and had it resolved in three minutes. What's she bitching about? Justin Wu: Playing by the book, I should walk you through everything we do at Ops. But today, I will challenge you with the hardest part of it. Justin Wu: You will see how you're not fit for Ops. Then I will let Ms. Yeh know.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭返回) >> <<#Simple(8)>> ->How do you know she's gonna listen? Justin Wu: She always listens to division leads. Justin Wu: Think about your field practice at Screening. Ms. Yeh considers Wayne's opinion when she gives the final verdict. <<#Simple(6)>> ->But you're not the lead of Ops. Justin Wu: Trust me, Ms. Yeh knows who's actually doing the work.;<< Play(吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_側站看外返回) >> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Plus, the actual division lead wants you even less.;<< Play(吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_側站看外) >> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->You don't have the guts to reject me outright? Justin Wu: I don't have the heart to reject you outright. I want you to see for yourself. Thought: My presence here is a waste of everyone's time... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->You can't do that. I have my rights. Justin Wu: You're absolutely right. This is unfair. Justin Wu: That is why I believe you deserve an honest answer. Justin Wu: Otherwise, I had other plans. <<#Simple(6)>> ->I had other plans to reject you without saying it. ->Well, thank you, kind sir. Justin Wu: Um... Justin Wu: OK. Now that I'm honest, I may as well go all the way. <<#GoTo(@嗯嗯講自己原本要放棄主角)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: In short, he can't do the job.;<< Play (羅龐安, MBU_羅龐安_正坐右看轉左) >> Justin Wu: Jesse...;<< Play(吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_側站看外返回) >> Jesse Lai: What? I'm saying it out loud for you.;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐看內返回) >> Jesse Lai: Being honest with him or not, it doesn't change anything.;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐抬手) >> Jesse Lai: If he can't do it, he can't do it. Justin Wu: ...;<< Play(吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_側站看外) >> Thought: I feel like I must say something...;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐抬手返回) >> <<#Simple(10)>> ->Hey, isn't there any position that doesn't need engineering or programming background? Justin Wu: As you can see, everyone here is either an engineer or a programmer, most with four or more years of experience. Jesse Lai: You know what? The division lead! No need for any background.;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐抬頭) >> Jesse Lai: As long as you know the right people at HQ.;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐抬頭返回) >> ->I get it now. Don't let me waste any more of your time. Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: I'm sorry, Jason. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Paxton Luo: What about rackers? I mean, all they do is send juicers up.;<< Play (羅龐安, MBU_羅龐安_正坐左看返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外) >> Jesse Lai: ...!;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐看外) >> Justin Wu: ...! Jesse Lai: That's an idea!;<< Play(吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_側站看外返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外返回) >> Paxton Luo: It's about racking time. If he's here for field practice, let him have a try. Justin Wu: Wait, we don't have opening for new rackers...;<< Play(吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_側站微慌) >> Paxton Luo: But we do need more, no? Paxton Luo: There's no opening only because the lead doesn't authorize hiring. Jesse Lai: He's making everyone's life miserable. Justin Wu: I wouldn't comment on that. But the bottom line is, I can't authorize an opening.;<< Play(吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_側站微慌返回) >> Jesse Lai: ...;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐看外返回) >> Jesse Lai: Hey, isn't Grandpa Li about to retire? Justin Wu: Yeah.;<< Play(吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭) >> Justin Wu: He did mention that. Jesse Lai: I thought the company's policy does allow us to recruit reserves when there is staff retirement imminent?;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐前傾) >> Justin Wu: Correct.;<< Play(吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭返回) >> Paxton Luo: Wow, you read the company's policy!;<< PlayUntil (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐前傾返回) >>;<< Play (羅龐安, MBU_羅龐安_正坐左看) >> Jesse Lai: I did my homework before applying for the job.;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐看外) >> Jesse Lai: Never walk into a place without knowing it front and back. My motto. Paxton Luo: Good for you.;<< Play (羅龐安, MBU_羅龐安_正坐左看返回) >> Justin Wu: OK. Let's do this, then.;<> Jesse Lai: Great!;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐看外返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Thank you, Jesse. Jesse Lai: Hee hee. Doesn't hurt to have more handsome guys around.;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐點頭) >> <<@胖胖邀功可以無是他>> Paxton Luo: I think I deserve a thank you, too?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外) >> <<#Simple(4)>> ->Thank you, too. Paxton Luo: Haha.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外返回) >> <<#GoTo(@不謝胖胖有點壞壞)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Paxton Luo: Hey, I'm talking to you! Jesse Lai: Haha, I'm starting to like him already.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外返回) >> <<@不謝胖胖有點壞壞>> ->Thank you, Packs.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外) >> Paxton Luo: Haha. Jesse Lai: Hee hee. Doesn't hurt to have more handsome guys around.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外返回) >> ->Thanks, Justin. Justin Wu: Don't celebrate yet. You are not officially hired. <<#GoTo(@胖胖邀功可以無是他)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> Justin Wu: Listen, Jason. Racker is not an easy job.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You mean, labor-intensive? Justin Wu: Physical labor won't be your primary concern. Justin Wu: The mental stress is enormous. ->I will try my best. Justin Wu: It's not about trying or not. Justin Wu: The mental stress is enormous. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Also, even if you're recruited as a reserve, you can't start as a racker right away.;<>;<> Justin Wu: There's an apprentice period, mentored by senior rackers.;<< Play (賴潔, MBU_賴潔_斜坐看內) >> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Can't you mentor me? Justin Wu: Racking is a specialized profession. Justin Wu: Also, racking is directly related to the safety of juicers. You must be mentored by real professionals. ->Thank you for everything. Justin Wu: My pleasure. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Since you may actually join Ops, there's one thing you should know.;<> Thought: A sudden change of attitude...?;<< Play (羅龐安, MBU_羅龐安_正坐右看返回) >> Justin Wu: At Ops, we generally agree that Silencio should be shut down, the sooner the better.;<> Thought: What!? Justin Wu: You're applying for a job that may not last very long. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Wait wait wait. Why would you want the farm to be shut down? // ->If the farm is shut down, you're all out of work. Justin Wu: ...You're correct. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Being the division that manages the Cellar, we know how bad things are.;<> Justin Wu: With equipment out of date and underserviced, we will see more and worse accidents in a couple of years. Justin Wu: We want the farm to be shut down, to avoid such casualties. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Is it an official decision already? Justin Wu: No, but the board of directors is considering it. ->Why would you hire people if you don't want this farm anymore? Justin Wu: Like I said. It's a decision made by Silencio. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Truth be told, we have no deciding power in the closure of the farm.;<> Justin Wu: However, we do what we can to facilitate it. Justin Wu: I know you have a hard time dealing with the idea, but this is the reality.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Does Screening agree with you? Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: They are against us. Justin Wu: But as to what they think about it, maybe you should ask them yourself. Justin Wu: It's not my place to make their point. ->Why are you telling me this now? Justin Wu: Because I don't want you to make uninformed decisions. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: ...I have done my duty to inform.;<> Justin Wu: Let's see which racker is free to oversee your field practice. Justin Wu: Feel free to walk around, or maybe talk to Jesse or Packs.;<> Justin Wu: Come find me when you're ready to start.;<> //俊恩走道他的座位打電話 //可以到走廊閒逛，但沒人帶主角離開的話離不開走廊和操作組。可以和操作組和走廊上的人聊天。 //可以和胖胖、賴潔、被賴潔搞的人(你和那女魔頭對話過了嗎？他很討厭吧？我想報復他，調查洋芋片可以搞到他)對話 //主現任物寫:操作組副組長要去找能帶我實作送駕流程的作業員。他特別強調必須是有資深資歷的人，不知道他為什麼那麼在意堅持這點。他說我可以先參觀一下環境等他回來。 <<#End>>

title: 操作失誤反應區
body:
<<@操作失誤反應區>>
//操作錯誤一次////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
<<#If($UpShelfWrongCount=1)>>
    Thought: ...!
    Justin Wu: Calm down. I understand this is your first time.
    Liam Hsu: Tsk.
<<#EndIf>>
//操作錯誤兩次
<<#If($UpShelfWrongCount=2)>>
    Liam Hsu: Hey!
    Liam Hsu: Focus!
<<#EndIf>>
//操作錯誤三次
<<#If($UpShelfWrongCount=3)>>
    Thought: Dammit, I...
    Liam Hsu: Oi! The heck are you doing!?
<<#EndIf>>
//操作錯誤四次
<<#If($UpShelfWrongCount=4)>>
    Thought: ...I screwed up.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站炒菜右看) >>
    Liam Hsu: Enough. You're an idiot.;<< Play (許世良, MBU_許世良_背站插腰右看) >>;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_背站左看) >>
    Liam Hsu: Let him down.;<< Play (吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_背站抱胸左看) >>
    Justin Wu: No. He has to finish it.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站炒菜右看返回) >>
    Thought: Judging by Justin's face, things are not going well...;<< Play (許世良, MBU_許世良_背站插腰右看返回) >>;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_背站左看返回) >>
    << PlayUntil (吳俊恩, MBU_吳俊恩_背站抱胸左看返回) >>
<<#EndIf>>
<<#If($UpShelfWrongCount>4)>>
    Justin Wu: ...
    Liam Hsu: ...
    Thought: They're all silent...
<<#EndIf>>
//操作錯誤十次
//許世良: 你在跟我他媽開玩笑嗎？
//許事量衝上去要打主角，被俊恩拉住
//吳俊恩: 戴士傑你下來吧，很明顯你不是和這工作。
//吳俊恩: 你可以回去了，操作組不會考慮收你這種人的。
//吳俊恩: 請你離開吧。
<<#If($ControllUpSheldState=1)>>
    <<#GoTo(@ControllUpSheldState1)>>
<<#EndIf>>
<<#If($ControllUpSheldState=2)>>
    <<#GoTo(@ControllUpSheldState2)>>
<<#EndIf>>
<<#If($ControllUpSheldState=3)>>
    <<#GoTo(@ControllUpSheldState3)>>
<<#EndIf>>
<<#If($ControllUpSheldState=4)>>
    <<#GoTo(@ControllUpSheldState4)>>
<<#EndIf>>
<<#If($ControllUpSheldState=5)>>
    <<#GoTo(@ControllUpSheldState5)>>
<<#EndIf>>
<<#If($ControllUpSheldState=6)>>
    <<#GoTo(@ControllUpSheldState6)>>
<<#EndIf>>
<<#If($ControllUpSheldState=7)>>
    <<#GoTo(@ControllUpSheldState7)>>
<<#EndIf>>
<<#If($ControllUpSheldState=8)>>
    <<#GoTo(@ControllUpSheldState8)>>
<<#EndIf>>
<<#If($ControllUpSheldState=9)>>
    <<#GoTo(@ControllUpSheldState9)>>
<<#EndIf>>

title: 玩家送架_第一人_第二步驟
body:
//////第一人;第二步驟///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
Golgotha Mk. III: Tranquilizer administered.
<<@ControllUpSheldState2>>
Golgotha Mk. III: Please give instruction.
<<Set($ControllUpSheldState,2)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>

    Golgotha Mk. III: <y>Error. Prerequisite procedure not completed.</y>;<<Set($UpShelfWrongCount,$UpShelfWrongCount+1)>>    
    <<#GoTo(@操作失誤反應區)>>

    ->Line 3.
    ->Line 4.

        <<#GoTo(@操作失誤反應區)>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
->[Instruction] Shock.
    Golgotha Mk. III: <y>Error. Target heart rate stable.</y>;<<Set($UpShelfWrongCount,$UpShelfWrongCount+1)>>    
    <<#GoTo(@操作失誤反應區)>>

    Golgotha Mk. III: <y>Error. Target already tranquilized.</y>;<<Set($UpShelfWrongCount,$UpShelfWrongCount+1)>>    
    <<#GoTo(@操作失誤反應區)>>
<<#EndSimple>>
<<#End>>

        << PlayUntil (許世良, MBU_許世良_背站插腰右看返回) >>
    <<#EndIf>>

title: 上車開出去一下以後2
body: //胖胖在那邊弄一陣子 Yale Yang: Dude, this is taking forever...;<> Paxton Luo: Working on it. Shut up. Yale Yang: ... Yale Yang: ...;<> Yale Yang: Hey, do you guys think that juicer will be OK? Paxton Luo: Absolutely.;<> Yale Yang: What about you, Mr. Dai? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He's probably gonna be OK. Yale Yang: I hope so. ->I'm not so optimistic. Yale Yang: ... <<#EndSimple>> Paxton Luo: Are you dumb? No one wants to talk about it. Not now. Yale Yang: Ah, right. I'm sorry!;<> //有拉拉桿 <<#If($DoPullEmergencyBar=true)>> Yale Yang: ... Yale Yang: ...;<> Yale Yang: How did you make up your mind? I mean, the emergency stop. Paxton Luo: Yale! Yale Yang: Sorry! I didn't mean to... Yale Yang: Please disregard if you mind… <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I don't mind. Yale Yang: Yeah. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Because it was the right thing to do. Yale Yang: Hey! Yale Yang: That is like suggesting the rest of us are cowards... <<#Simple(8)>> ->I didn't mean that. Sorry. Yale Yang: It's alright. I was just saying. Haha. ->Hesitating when someone's life is at stake? That is cowardice. Yale Yang: ... Yale Yang: ... Yale Yang: Probably... Thought: Yale looks hurt. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Paxton Luo: Shut up, Yale! ->Because I couldn't fail him. Yale Yang: Who? The juicer? Yale Yang: I see what you mean... Yale Yang: How would his family feel? Yale Yang: You're really brave. ->Because I don't have no family. Just a girlfriend. Yale Yang: Not married? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not yet. Yale Yang: Not yet, but on the agenda, huh? ->Nope. <<#EndSimple>> Paxton Luo: That lever could have ruined your wedding. Paxton Luo: What girl in her right mind would marry a mountain of debt? Yale Yang: Packs! No one wants to talk about that! Paxton Luo: Oops, my bad... Paxton Luo: Sorry, Jason. <<#Simple(6)>> ->It's OK. Paxton Luo: Huh, um... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Paxton Luo: ... <<#EndSimple>> ->I'd rather not talk about it. Yale Yang: I see... Yale Yang: Sorry. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: ... <<#EndIf>> //沒拉拉桿 <<#If($DoPullEmergencyBar=false)>> Yale Yang: ... Yale Yang: ...;<> Yale Yang: Do you regret it, Jason? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What? ->Regret? For what? Yale Yang: For not pulling the emergency stop. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. I can't bet my whole life on it. <<#GoTo(@不會，我知道自己不能拿人生去賭。)>> ->A little bit. <<#GoTo(@有點後悔。)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->No. I can't bet my whole life on it. <<@不會，我知道自己不能拿人生去賭。>> Yale Yang: I see...;<> Yale Yang: But if you did pull the lever, maybe... <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Are you blaming me? Yale Yang: No! I just... ->Maybe I'll end up under a mountain of debt. Yale Yang: R-right... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->A little bit. <<@有點後悔。>> Yale Yang: I figured. <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: I've been mentally preparing myself for that moment, but never quite ready… Thought: I'm not doing any better... <<#EndIf>> Paxton Luo: Enough! Stop bothering him! Yale Yang: OK. Paxton Luo: ... Paxton Luo: Ha! It's done! Yale Yang: Awesome!;<>;//胖胖把平版收起來 Paxton Luo: Let's get going!;<> <><> <<#End>>

title: 操作組結束回篩選組
body: //大家先問操作組長怎樣？采欣有點賭氣說他不是跟大家說過了嗎？大家笑他他很部會描述。 //玩家可以選描述清楚，或是一樣跟簡單。 //然後大家問所以主角順利嗎？發生什麼事？ //大家先七嘴八舌猜主角的遭遇 //玩家說出發生的事情以後氣氛變很壓抑，玩家可以決定要說多係(小峰會說不想說可以不用說。球球鼓勵說)，說最淺是出意外，有人受傷。說最細是主角拉桿都講。 //有沒有錄取也可以提。 //講完以後大家的感想是下面的狀況比想像的遭，但還是拒絕相信操作組，只覺得他們是避重就輕。而且自視甚高。 //然後小峰邀請曹泰銘等一下來他座為幫忙。 //最後廖哥近來，嫌大家抓到機會就一直聊天。(這裡做一個差異是小峰有沒有跟廖哥和好，沒合好:廖哥一講話，小峰直接走掉。和好:看支線寫怎樣決定。) //廖哥提到經理回來了，他心情似乎不太好，大家皮繃緊一點。 //結束以後泰銘去他座位正站跟他討論事情，玩家可以主動跟他們對話亂選人的事情。 //自由行動可以問廖哥那些特勤是在幹嘛？廖哥會說不清楚，但應該是調查機密洩漏。沒做虧心事配合就是了。 //要提到亂送人下去，篩選組的說法是他們明明可以調整參數，但他們硬是用錯誤的把人送上去 Quentin Qiu: Eh!?;<> Quentin Qiu: Jayster's back! <> Jeff Wu: Woo-hoo!;<> Quentin Qiu: How does Ops feel? Bunch of snobs, eh?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not too bad. ->Actually, they're pretty nice. Quentin Qiu: Aren't they, like, self-righteous, snobby? <<@球球說操作組都會說的白爛話>> Quentin Qiu: For example, like...;<> Jeff Wu: "Leave it to the real professionals.";<> Quentin Qiu: Or, "Anyone with a mouth could do your job at Screening.";<> Thought: They are kinda like that, more or less...;<> ->Yeah, it didn't feel good. ->They are snobby, alright. Quentin Qiu: Told you! Quentin Qiu: Did they give you the usual treatment? <<#GoTo(@球球說操作組都會說的白爛話)>> <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: Speaking of, what is it like at Ops?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What do you mean? ->Which part? Jeff Wu: Their office. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: I've never been there. Haven't even seen pictures of it.;<> Quentin Qiu: Yeah, right. Totally no idea. Cathy Hsieh: Eh? Haven't I told you already?;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: You did, but there's hardly any information... <> Cathy Hsieh: Eh!? Cathy Hsieh: I told you everything I saw! Quentin Qiu: A dark, dark place.;<> Jeff Wu: Many screens.;<> Quentin Qiu: How are we supposed to get any insight about that!;<> Cathy Hsieh: But those are facts! Quentin Qiu: Come on, Jayster! Do a better job than she did.;<>;<> <<@請主角描述操作組問題集>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->There's a lot of blue lighting. ($DescibeControlSayBlueLight) Quentin Qiu: ... Jeff Wu: ... Quentin Qiu: Okay! Okay! Stop!;<> Quentin Qiu: Never mind! Quentin Qiu: You're a male version of Cathy.;<> Cathy Hsieh: Hey!;<> <> ->They had very, very high ceiling. ($DescibeControlCeiling) <> <> <> Jeff Wu: Wow, sounds classy. Quentin Qiu: Ops wins this round. Our ceiling is meh. Quentin Qiu: And? <<#GoTo(@請主角描述操作組問題集)>> ->There's an elevator that goes to the control room for racking. ($DescibeControlElevator) <> <> <> Quentin Qiu: Elevator, right in their office? Jeff Wu: Cooool. Jeff Wu: Anything else? <<#GoTo(@請主角描述操作組問題集)>> ->There's a HUGE glass pane that turns into a screen. ($DescibeControlGlass) <> <> <> Quentin Qiu: Dayum! Jeff Wu: How huge is HUGE? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not sure. Didn't bring a tape measure with me. Jeff Wu: Yeah, you're right. ->About 6 or 7 of these desks together side by side. Quentin Qiu: That's HUGE! <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: Sounds expensive... Quentin Qiu: That's it? <<#GoTo(@請主角描述操作組問題集)>> ->That's about the size of it. ($DescribeControlCount) <<#If($DescribeControlCount=1)>> Quentin Qiu: Come on? Give us one more? Quentin Qiu: Boring. <<#EndIf>> <<#If($DescribeControlCount>1)>> Jeff Wu: Wow! Quentin Qiu: It's still hard to imagine. Quentin Qiu: But at least now we're sure it's very different from our average offices. <<#EndIf>> <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: You guys, stop bothering him!;<> Quentin Qiu: Why? Say, Tyler, you're surprisingly uninterested in Ops.;<> Tyler Cao: He's there for field practice, not a tour. Quentin Qiu: Oh! Right!;<> Quentin Qiu: Tell us what happened there, Jayster!;<>;<> Tyler Cao: Hey... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->We had a very bad accident. <<@跟球球小峰提起地窖意外>> Quentin Qiu: Accident? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Widespread malfunction in the Cellar. Juicers were hurt. Jeff Wu: ...Sounds bad indeed.;<> ->The machine went nuts when I was doing racking. Jeff Wu: ...Sounds bad indeed.;<> Jeff Wu: Was anyone hurt? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes, but I'm not sure how many. Jeff Wu: Geez. ->I don't know. <<#EndSimple>> ->System failure, and stuff. <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: ...;<> Quentin Qiu: What did I say? They're not doing their job.;<> Jeff Wu: Yeah. And the assault yesterday is their fault, too...;<> <<#Simple(12)>> ->Wait. You really think they're not doing their job? Quentin Qiu: Of course?;<> Quentin Qiu: They have one job, which is to keep the Cellar in order. Now it's a total mess! Jeff Wu: How else, do you think, that things get this bad in the Cellar?;<> <<#GoTo(@主角開始跟球球小峰解釋操作組立場)>> ->I don't think it's their fault. Quentin Qiu: What? Jeff Wu: How can it not be their fault? <<@主角開始跟球球小峰解釋操作組立場>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->They don't have the budget to keep the Cellar well-serviced. ->They said HQ doesn't want them to. Jeff Wu: Ha! Can't believe they're saying that...;<> Thought: ...????? Quentin Qiu: These people at Ops take the highest salary. That's how they spent their budget. Tyler Cao: ...Are you aware of this, Jason?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Is it wrong to have higher salary? Tyler Cao: Normally, no. But each division has a fixed budget. ->I don't know. Tyler Cao: Let me explain. ->What does their salary have to do with the budget? Quentin Qiu: A LOT! Tyler Cao: Let me explain. <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: Silencio receives a fixed budget from HQ every quarter.;<>;<> Tyler Cao: The farm generates a more or less fixed revenue. HQ has no reason to increase our budget. Tyler Cao: A small part of this budget is for routine expenditure. The rest is allocated to divisions. We decide how to spend the money. Jeff Wu: Yes. We have a lot of power in managing our own division.;<> Jeff Wu: They decided to use most of their budget on staff salary, instead of keeping the Cellar serviced. Quentin Qiu: Even if HQ decides to increase the overall budget, they'd use the surplus for their own salary.;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: Don't believe their story, Jason. Tyler Cao: Yeah. Can't find one ounce of empathy across the whole Ops team. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see. Quentin Qiu: That's what we think about them in general... ->I don't think they're bad people. Quentin Qiu: We didn't say they're bad people. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Personally, I hate them with a passion. But I wouldn't call them bad people.;<> Quentin Qiu: Their issue, like Tyler said, is that they have no empathy. Quentin Qiu: They never consider how others feel. Ego-centric.;<> Quentin Qiu: They believe they deserve better pay, on par with what their counterparts in other farms are getting. Quentin Qiu: Is that wrong? No.;<> Quentin Qiu: Without context, their salary is totally justified. Quentin Qiu: But with context, more staff salary means less budget for everything else.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I still don't think it's their fault. Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: I'm not really blaming them. ->But HQ could afford to increase our budget, right? Quentin Qiu: They can, but Silencio can't provide much more return for their investment. Quentin Qiu: HQ has no reason to increase budgets. They know that. Thought: They did mention something about this... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Like I said, I wouldn't call them bad people.;<> Quentin Qiu: I agree that these accidents are fundamentally Vision's fault, as a company. Quentin Qiu: Us employees can't be held responsible. Quentin Qiu: But again, like I said...;<> Quentin Qiu: They could have kept things serviced and running, at the cost of their own salary.;<> Quentin Qiu: But they chose not to do it.;<> Quentin Qiu: They had this option to take more responsibility and help people. A lot of people. Quentin Qiu: They gave it up.;<> Jeff Wu: Worse still, they mock us for doing so.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Mock us? Quentin Qiu: They call us lofty, impractical, dreamers. ->Would you take this responsibility, though? Quentin Qiu: Let me make it clear. I don't think they are doing anything wrong.;<> Quentin Qiu: They're not obliged to take on extra responsibilities. Quentin Qiu: Then, back to your question.;<> Quentin Qiu: Most of us at Screening have chosen the opposite. Quentin Qiu: We spent most of budget on contributor benefits. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Sometimes when the budget is too low, Wayne would ask us whether we'd like to forfeit our own bonus. Quentin Qiu: It's an anonymous process, so I can't tell who decided to help the contributors at their own cost. Tyler Cao: But judging by the numbers, most of us did.;<> Quentin Qiu: And don't forget our salary and bonus are way lower than theirs. Quentin Qiu: Anyway, Screening and Ops have chosen different paths.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->They mock you for that? Tyler Cao: Maybe not exactly mocking, but they do think we're idealistic idiots. Quentin Qiu: Now you know why we don't like them? ->Personally, I think you guys are a bit too…idealistic. Quentin Qiu: Maybe. Although... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: We just want to make the world a better place.;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: And we didn't ask everyone else to do the same. Quentin Qiu: How could they be so mean to us?;<> <> Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: I...;<> Quentin Qiu: Don't tell me you're taking their side again.;<> Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: I'm not... Tyler Cao: Cathy, sometimes not taking sides is taking sides.;<> Cathy Hsieh: I haven't said anything yet! Quentin Qiu: Anyway, that is the situation.;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: Hey, why are we talking about this again?;<> Tyler Cao: You guys led the subject off course. Quentin Qiu: Haha, right! Sorry! <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: Enough of this gloomy talk. Quentin Qiu: Yeah, you're right. ->Nothing interesting. Quentin Qiu: Huh? Was it that boring? <<#GoTo(@你呆喔，只是去工作哪會有什麼勁爆)>> ->What do you want to hear? Quentin Qiu: The exciting part! <<@你呆喔，只是去工作哪會有什麼勁爆>> Jeff Wu: What's exciting about work, silly?;<>;<> Jeff Wu: Just tell us what you did down there.;<> Jeff Wu: If you don't mind.;<> <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I had some hands-on experience with racking. Jeff Wu: Ah! Is it hard? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not really. I think I did well. Quentin Qiu: Awesome. //if表現好 <<#If($UpShelfWrongCount<3)>> Thought: Yeah, I did pretty well. <<#EndIf>> //if表現不好 <<#If($UpShelfWrongCount>2)>> Thought: Would they call it "well"...? <<#EndIf>> ->Very hard. Quentin Qiu: Seriously? Jeff Wu: It's his first day. Of course. Quentin Qiu: You're right. <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: Anything else? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That's about it. Quentin Qiu: Huh. ->And we need cars to get around in the Cellar. Quentin Qiu: Cathy did mention the cars.;<> Quentin Qiu: Are they comfy? Thought: ...Not too shabby? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Pretty comfy, I think. Quentin Qiu: Niiice. ->Nope. Quentin Qiu: Really? ->How do you define "comfy"? Quentin Qiu: The kind like you sink right into the seat. Oooo, comfy. Thought: ...Huh? <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: You've bothered Jason enough, QQ!;<> Quentin Qiu: Yeah, yeah, you're boring, Tyler. <> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Then there was a bad accident. <<#GoTo(@跟球球小峰提起地窖意外)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->We had a very bad accident. <<#GoTo(@跟球球小峰提起地窖意外)>> ->I'd rather not talk about it. Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: But— Jeff Wu: Don't push him, QQ.;<> Quentin Qiu: But I'm curious!;<> <> <<#Simple(6)>> ->We had a very bad accident. <<#GoTo(@跟球球小峰提起地窖意外)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: Okay... Quentin Qiu: I won't push you, Jayster. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <> <> George Liao: Cutting slack again, huh.;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: Mr. Liao!?;<>;<> George Liao: Quentin, how is your assignment going?;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: Almost ready!;<> George Liao: Ms. Yeh is back. If anyone needs to see her, she's in the office. George Liao: Not in a good mood, though. Don't say I didn't warn you. Tyler Cao: Why? George Liao: Don't know. Probably has something to do with the IIB. Thought: The IIB? Those people in black suits? George Liao: Jeff...;<> //if關係處理好了 // 放心，東西我都準備好了，放在你桌上了。 // ……… // 你今天很準時啊？ // 總不能老是讓你替我操心啦。 // ……嗯，很好。 // 想法: 這阿伯居然笑了。 // 繼續保持。 // 好啦、好啦，我會啦！ // <> // ……… // ……… // ……是我的錯覺嗎？ // 你跟廖哥的關係變好了欸，小峰？ // ……是有好一點啦。 // 不過也是多虧士傑跟隆生就是了。 // ->願意接納廖哥還是你自己做到的。 // 哈哈，盡力啦。 // 畢竟他也說他願意嘗試嘛。 // ->主要是隆生的功勞。 // 謙虛王欸，哈哈。 // ->這沒什麼。 // 嘿，對我來說可是非常有什麼喔！ // ->……… // //if沒處理小峰支線 Jeff Wu: Don't chase. It's on track.;<> Jeff Wu: I'm busy with something else. Your assignment comes next.;<> George Liao: You... Jeff Wu: The deadline is pretty far away, no? Jeff Wu: Don't I get to decide my own priorities? George Liao: ... George Liao: Whatever suits you. <> // Jeff Wu: Tyler, can I borrow you for a minute?;<> Tyler Cao: What for?;<>;<> Jeff Wu: Could you come to my desk?;<> Tyler Cao: Sure. <> Quentin Qiu: Gotta work, Jayster. Or Mr. Liao will kill me.;<> Quentin Qiu: Anything else you want to share?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. Good luck with the work. Quentin Qiu: You bet! ->Is there anyone sending unqualified candidates down, to meet their assigned quota? Quentin Qiu: ...What? Quentin Qiu: You mean, someone in our division? Thought: I seem to have hit a nerve. Quentin Qiu: Why ask? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->They told me so at Ops. Quentin Qiu: Bastards... ->There's this rumor. Quentin Qiu: For real? <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: As far as I know, no one would do that. Quentin Qiu: Wait, there's actually someone.;<> Quentin Qiu: Sky could totally have done it. But he's fired now. Thought: More like transferred... Quentin Qiu: I don't know enough to answer your question, since I'm not involved in consultation.;<> Quentin Qiu: You could try asking Jeff or Tyler. Quentin Qiu: Okay, I've got work to do. Quentin Qiu: Talk to you later. <<#EndSimple>> <>;<> <<#End>>

title: 跟楊冶平尷尬搭電梯2
body: Yale Yang: ... Yale Yang: ...;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看) >> Yale Yang: You're not the talkative type?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. Yale Yang: I see. ->Depends on who I'm talking to. Yale Yang: Ah... Yale Yang: Is that so? ->Feeling bored? Yale Yang: No, not really... <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: ... Yale Yang: Hey...;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看返回) >> Yale Yang: After everything that happened today, I guess you won't want to join us, right...? //被說不能錄取 <<#If($UpShelfWrongCount>3)>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Justin said he didn't want me. Yale Yang: Yeah, he did. ->I'd like to join you if I could. ->Yeah. Too much stress. Yale Yang: I see... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //被說可以錄取 <<#If($UpShelfWrongCount<4)>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. Too much stress. ->I haven't decided yet. ->I think I will. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Yale Yang: I see...;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站低頭) >> Yale Yang: Remember what you saw here, if you end up being assigned to Screening. Yale Yang: I'm not like Mr. Hsu. I think Screening simply doesn't understand what's happening here.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站低頭返回) >> Yale Yang: The company's too obsessed with confidentiality. We need more cross-division exchange. Yale Yang: Right now there are too many misunderstandings.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站抬頭) >> Yale Yang: We have been dealing with the toughest problems of this farm.;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看返回) >> Yale Yang: The Screening team, they don't see what we do. They just make unreasonable demands.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站抬頭返回) >> Yale Yang: You saw it yourself. Two casualties in one day.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看) >> Yale Yang: I guess life at Screening must be cozy?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看) >> <<#Simple(999999)>> ->There was an assault incident yesterday. Yale Yang: What??;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看驚訝) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Assault in the contract rooms. One dead, one injured. ->A lunatic was stabbing people. <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: Gosh... Yale Yang: Did they find out the cause? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He came here for revenge. ->His family died in the Cellar. <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: Now they should have learned a lesson.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看驚訝返回) >> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Learned a lesson? ->What are you talking about? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: Please.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看低頭) >> Yale Yang: Don't send unqualified juicers down. No more. <<@小楊討論幹嘛篩選亂送人下來>> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Who? Screening? Yale Yang: Who else? ->What do you mean by "unqualified"? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: They send people obviously not fit for juicing all the time. Yale Yang: It doesn't take a doctor to tell. They're walking bombs, accidents waiting to happen. Yale Yang: And in many accidents that did happen, the juicers are not fit for juicing, either. Yale Yang: If they kept up their screening standards, a lot of these could have been avoided! Yale Yang: Since you've been working with them for a while... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Just one day. Yale Yang: I know. Still, you did work with them. ->Hold on. This is not my fault. Yale Yang: I'm not trying to blame you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: Did they ever say why they are sending unqualified people down?;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看低頭返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sorry, I don't know. Yale Yang: No need to be sorry! I wasn't really expecting anything. ->I suppose it's because those people desperately need a job. Yale Yang: Job!? Yale Yang: Sending them down here to be tortured, do they call it a job? <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: Initially I thought they were just lazy asses. Yale Yang: Then I heard they're doing it on purpose, to meet assigned quotas.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看返回) >> Yale Yang: More juicers signed means more bonus, right?;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看) >> Thought: I don't think it's a case of the more the better... Thought: But at Screening, they do have a minimum quota to meet... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->As far as I know, yes. ->They do have quotas to meet. Yale Yang: I knew it... ->I believe they just want to help those in need. Yale Yang: Exposing them to risks of injury and death. Is that helping? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Maybe they have no idea how bad things are down here. Yale Yang: That is unrelated. They just have to uphold their own standards.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看低頭) >> Yale Yang: We have written standards for assessing juicers. Yale Yang: Many of these accidents could have been avoided, if they followed the rules. Thought: He's right... <<#GoTo(@楊冶平覺得對他們不公平耶)>> ->But without a job, they'd die anyway. Yale Yang: ...;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看低頭) >> Yale Yang: I don't believe it. There must be alternatives. <<@楊冶平覺得對他們不公平耶>>> Yale Yang: No matter what, sending the unqualified down is not a solution.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看低頭返回) >> Yale Yang: It's unfair to the juicers, and to us, too.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看返回) >> Yale Yang: We have to watch the same tragedy happen over and over again. Thought: If what I saw today happens on a regular basis, well...;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看) >> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Thought: ... Thought: Slipping unqualified candidates through...;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看返回) >> Thought: Jeff and Tyler, did they do it...? ->Cozier than here. Yale Yang: Thought so. Yale Yang: ... Yale Yang: I have this question that has been bugging me for a long time. Yale Yang: Why do they keep sending unqualified juicers down? <<#GoTo(@小楊討論幹嘛篩選亂送人下來)>> <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: ... Yale Yang: *Sigh* Never mind...;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看返回) >> Yale Yang: I can't dwell on these negative thoughts. Yale Yang: Poor juicers.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站低頭) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看) >> Thought: That reminds me... Thought: The people at Ops, they have no problem with the word "juicer" at all...;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站低頭返回) >> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You guys don't seem to mind the word "juicer". Yale Yang: Eh? Do they really mind the word at Screening?;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看) >> Yale Yang: We don't have that hypocrite culture here. Yale Yang: All they do is talk and be nice. We are actually dealing with life and death situations here. <<#Simple(8)>> ->They do more than talk. ->Hey, that is unfair. Yale Yang: Okay, I admit I don't know what exactly they do at Screening. Yale Yang: Isn't it just talking to the juicers? Yale Yang: My point is... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Yale Yang: We care more about juicers than they do. Care with action.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看低頭) >> Yale Yang: The Screening people do their fancy talk, then leave the rest to us. Yale Yang: Do you think the use of a word really matters?;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看低頭返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You're right. Yale Yang: Yeah.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看返回) >> ->The contributors may not like the word. Yale Yang: It's their own problem!;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看返回) >> Yale Yang: Our job is to make sure they're safe, not to entertain them. <<#Simple(8)>> ->This is about social etiquette. Yale Yang: Um...;<< PlayUntil (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看) >> Yale Yang: I know. I wouldn't use the word if they clearly don't like it.;<< Play (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看返回) >> ->You're right. Yale Yang: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->That is encouraging discrimination. Yale Yang: It's just a word. It means what you want it to mean. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It's a loaded word. Yale Yang: Alright. I respect our differences. ->You're right. <<#EndSimple>> << PlayUntil (楊冶平, MBU_楊冶平_正站左看返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> ->Life ain't easy for anyone. Yale Yang: Right... ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>>

title: 調查物件:接駁車告示牌
問晉峰有為業績亂放人嗎？
body: Jeff Wu: Jason? What's the matter? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I heard some of us might be letting unqualified candidates through, to meet quotas. <<@開始理解篩選組亂放人的謠言>> Thought: They're both frowning. Tyler Cao: Heard... From Ops? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. How did you know? Tyler Cao: Thought so. ->No. Why?;<> <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: I heard them spread rumors like that before.;<> Tyler Cao: Plus, you just came back from Ops. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Ah. ->What do you think? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: ...;<> Tyler Cao: Simply put, it's a rumor, groundless. Jeff Wu: Agreed. I'm not aware of anyone doing that.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->They could be doing it in secret. Tyler Cao: No. It's pointless. Thought: Pointless...? ->Meaning someone is fabricating stories? Tyler Cao: More like contributing to untruthful hearsay. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: It's true that we have a minimum quota for the number of contributors signed.;<> Tyler Cao: But there is no bonus for signing extras. <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see. // ->That still makes a valid motive for letting unqualified candidates in. Jeff Wu: True. I heard Jade is having trouble meeting her quota.;<> Jeff Wu: But that is because she's taken too many sick days. Tyler Cao: And she will meet the quota in a couple of days. Tyler Cao: Normally, it's pretty hard to not meet the quota.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: On the other hand, we get warnings for signing up contributors of low efficiency. Tyler Cao: There's really nothing to gain from signing up unqualified candidates. Jeff Wu: Right! <<#Simple(8)>> ->So, where did this rumor come from? Jeff Wu: Most likely from Ops. //if說不是操作組 <<#If($DLState=跟穩哥小峰說不是從操作組聽來的)>> Tyler Cao: ...You really didn't hear it from them? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Alright, in fact I did. Tyler Cao: I knew it. Jeff Wu: *Sigh* ->No. Tyler Cao: Good. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> ->I get your point. Sorry for doubting. Jeff Wu: No offense taken. Jeff Wu: You're too new to know what's what. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: The rumor is reasonable, since we do keep pushing the bottom line of screening standards.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What bottom line? Jeff Wu: Physical exams, and stuff.;<> Jeff Wu: Wait! Before you ask! Jeff Wu: We push the bottom line, but still follow the rules.;<> Jeff Wu: We only make occasional exceptions for those who come slightly below the bar. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Isn't that dangerous? Jeff Wu: Not at all. ->Why do you do that? Jeff Wu: ...You know some of them really need this job. We are their last hope. Jeff Wu: Sometimes we let them pass, as long as they're reasonably fit. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Do you get any benefit out of it? Jeff Wu: ...You know some of them really needs this job. We are their last hope. Jeff Wu: Sometimes we let them pass, as long as they're reasonably fit. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Isn't that dangerous? Jeff Wu: Wait! Before you ask!;<> Jeff Wu: We push the bottom line, but still follow the rules. Jeff Wu: We only make occasional exceptions for those who come slightly below the bar.;<> ->You're saying, this is entirely for helping people? Jeff Wu: Yes, without exposing them to additional risks. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: I understand it may increase the burden of Ops.;<> Jeff Wu: Huh?;<> Tyler Cao: Yeah. Cathy told me they decide generating power based on the physical condition of each contributor.;<> Tyler Cao: All contributors meeting the standard can be sent to the main network. Tyler Cao: Exceptions must be evaluated on a case-by-case basis. Tyler Cao: You remember what Cathy said, no? Engineers hate exceptions. Jeff Wu: They are complaining about doing extra work? Tyler Cao: Probably. Jeff Wu: OK, they have a legit reason. But we can't leave those poor people out in the cold! Tyler Cao: Screening and Ops have differences. No need to argue over it. Jeff Wu: I agree. Jeff Wu: Anyway, that's it, Jason. Rumor.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I wouldn't call it entirely rumor. Jeff Wu: *Sigh* Jeff Wu: Yeah, I guess you can say that. Jeff Wu: My point is, no one at Screening is making these exceptions for personal gains. Jeff Wu: The system is specially designed to avoid that. Tyler Cao: Right. ->Thank you for the explanation. Jeff Wu: You're welcome! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: Now, we have work to do, if you don't mind. <> <<#End>> ->Nothing. Don't let me keep you. Jeff Wu: O...kay? Jeff Wu: Ask away if you have any questions. We are not that busy right now. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nothing, really. Jeff Wu: I see. Talk to you later. <<#End>> ->I heard some of us might be letting unqualified candidates through, to meet quotas. <<#GoTo(@開始理解篩選組亂放人的謠言)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>>

title: 俊恩走掉阿緯反應
body: //4.沒有提到俊恩所以阿偉跟過來 <<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=4)>> Wayne Zheng: What's this about? Wayne Zheng: Why didn't you tell me Justin is here? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I thought we three should have a talk, together. Wayne Zheng: You should have asked for my opinion!;<> ->Sorry, I forgot to. Wayne Zheng: Forgot!?;<> Wayne Zheng: Do you think I'd buy that? <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I don't know what it is that you want. Now Justin is even more uncooperative now. Wayne Zheng: I don't know what his problem is. He's now seeing me as an enemy!;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I didn't know things are that bad between you two. Wayne Zheng: Now you know. ->Sorry, I only wanted to help. Wayne Zheng: *Sigh* ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: What happened, happened.;<> Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: Sorry for being mad at you. You couldn't have known he's so unreasonable.;<> Wayne Zheng: He wasn't like this before. Now he's as stubborn as a mule. Wayne Zheng: I guess I'm not doing much better, with Justin giving me hell, on top of everything else.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->What kind of "everything else"? Wayne Zheng: That is the subject of our talk today. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //3.有提到俊恩所以阿偉跟過來 <<#If($TopFloorStiuationPath=3)>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: *Sigh*;<> Wayne Zheng: Sorry for that episode. That was awkward. Wayne Zheng: I thought maybe he finally wants to talk, this time. Wayne Zheng: Turns out I was wrong. Too good to be true.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->He did say he didn't want to see you. Wayne Zheng: What!? Wayne Zheng: Why didn't you tell me? Why brought me here?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It felt like a good idea, to bring you along... Wayne Zheng: A good idea, ha!;<> Wayne Zheng: ... <> ->I didn't know things are that bad between you two. Wayne Zheng: You didn't know Justin Wu is such a stubborn mule. <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Next time, ask me before making decisions, okay? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I promise. Wayne Zheng: Thank you.;<> ->Sorry, I can't promise that. Wayne Zheng: Why? It's a simple request.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Next time, things could be different. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Then I'll think twice every time you propose something. Thought: He's not as angry as I thought? ->OK. Wayne Zheng: Thank you. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: Now, let's talk about what I brought you up here for. Thought: He's getting serious all of a sudden...;<> <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: Let's talk over there. <<#End>>

title: 等到吳俊恩修完
body:
//在園地等吳俊恩修完，他時間到會開始前往停車場
Justin Wu: Sorry for keeping you waiting.
Justin Wu: Let's go.
<<#Wait(2)>>
Justin Wu: First thing in the Cellar, grab a car.
Thought: Car...?
<<#Simple(6)>>
->What do we need a car for?
    Justin Wu: I don't know how big you think the Cellar is. Take that number and multiply by ten.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: The Cellar is a gigantic structure with multiple shuttle stops. We get around in these cars.
Justin Wu: Also, being so deep beneath the surface, the air in the Cellar is slightly radioactive.
Justin Wu: Not exactly a hazard yet, but you don't want to be exposed.
<<#End>>

body:

->Hi.

->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Nathan Chuang: I'd like to have a talk, if you don't mind.
Nathan Chuang: You don't want to miss what I have to say. Trust me.
Nathan Chuang: So, can I borrow you for a minute?;<< Play (莊智宣, MBU_莊智宣_側站低頭插腰) >>
Thought: Despite the gangster look, he sounds like an educated guy.
<<#Simple(99999)>>

    Nathan Chuang: Absolutely.
->Do I know you?
    Nathan Chuang: Not yet.
    Nathan Chuang: We will get to know each other very soon.
    Thought: ...Huh?
->OK.
    Nathan Chuang: Very cooperative, my friend!
    Nathan Chuang: Good, good.
<<#EndSimple>>
Nathan Chuang: You won't regret this, you know.
Nathan Chuang: What do you have to lose, right?;<< Play (莊智宣, MBU_莊智宣_側站低頭插腰返回) >>
Thought: What is this about...?
Thought: He'd better not waste my time.
<<#End>>

title: 地窖電梯導覽2
body: //進入中控區 Justin Wu: Now we are passing through Central Control.;<> Justin Wu: It's a giant relay station. Silencio routes all resources through it.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Resources, like what? Justin Wu: Mostly raw materials needed by factories. <<#GoTo(@俊恩解釋中控區域的作用)>> ->Relay station? Justin Wu: Relay station of supplies, mostly raw materials. <<@俊恩解釋中控區域的作用>> Justin Wu: Each flop farm is connected to three to five fully automated factories, serving as their shared brain.;<>;<> Justin Wu: These factories produce all sorts of things, from parts to retail products. Silencio controls several steel mills and automobile assembly plants. Justin Wu: With Silencio coordinating, these factories can maximize their efficiency. Justin Wu: Amazing, isn't it? Industry augmented by internet on steroids.;<> Thought: Silencio has purposes other than flop farming...?;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Central Control is fully automated, only needing human intervention for occasional servicing. Justin Wu: This is part of Logistics' domains. They keep the entire place serviced.;<> Justin Wu: I heard the Flop Core is located here, too.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->"Heard"? Justin Wu: The Flop Core is THE top secret of this farm.;<> Justin Wu: Most people, me included, don't even know its exact location. Justin Wu: A friend at Logistics told me inspectors from HQ take this elevator when they do routine check on the Flop Core.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->What is the Flop Core? <<@俊嗯嗯說明運算核心是啥咪>> Justin Wu: ...;<> Thought: Is he trying to come up with a shorter explanation? Justin Wu: The Flop Core is the brain of this farm, so to speak.;<> Justin Wu: It's in charge of things to be done, how they should be done, so on and so forth. Justin Wu: The Flop Core is powered by juicers, who provide the raw flops.;<> Justin Wu: It draws from this raw power and use it to perform various functions.;<> Thought: In other words, an omni-purpose butler who's also its own boss...? Justin Wu: The Flop Core doesn't have a dedicated name. We all call it by the moniker of the farm. Justin Wu: Silencio.;<>;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->What is the Flop Core? <<#GoTo(@俊嗯嗯說明運算核心是啥咪)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Back to the Central Control. Justin Wu: We can't feel it inside the elevator, but these parts are super hot.;<> Justin Wu: If this door opens right now, the heat wave will be enough to grill us alive. <<#Simple(8)>> ->But you said Logistics performs services here? Justin Wu: Yes, it's a job with occupational hazards.;<> Justin Wu: The heat is not a big problem, if you are protected by proper equipment. <<@俊嗯嗯說防護都快過期啦>> Justin Wu: But according to my friend, they haven't replaced protective gear in years.;<> Thought: He sounds very concerned. Justin Wu: Pretty much all divisions are facing the same situation... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Including Ops?;////////////////////////////////////////////廢廠話題////////////////////////////////////////////////////// Justin Wu: Especially Ops. <<@俊恩透過省防護服暗示廢場>> Justin Wu: This might have to do with HQ's recent plans. It's only my own guess, of course. Thought: HQ's plans? ->Like, to cut cost? Justin Wu: Cutting costs is a must. However... <<#GoTo(@俊恩透過省防護服暗示廢場)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Where were we again? Oh, right, the heat near Central Control.;<> ->How hot exactly is it? Grill us alive? Justin Wu: ... Thought: He seems caught a bit unprepared. People seldom ask about this, I guess. Justin Wu: Honestly, I don't know for sure.;<> Justin Wu: Maybe we will melt down into two puddles of grease...? Thought: Melt down...? Justin Wu: No, can't be that hot.;<> Justin Wu: Let's just say it's really bad for our health. The Logistics team put on protective suits when they work here. Justin Wu: ...;<> <<#GoTo(@俊嗯嗯說防護都快過期啦)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: To solve the heat issue, the designer of this farm used sea water for cooling.;<>;<> Justin Wu: Until Silencio came around, overheating had been plaguing all flop farms. Justin Wu: Sea water channeled inside is heated and evaporated. The steam is used to generate power. Justin Wu: The design is very efficient. The farm is largely self-sufficient.;<> Thought: He's almost proud of it.;<> Justin Wu: Back then, Silencio represented the state of the art. Justin Wu: Fast forward to now, though, everything is outdated.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Is it that bad? Justin Wu: Hard as we try, we are barely keeping it running. ->Why don't you give it a thorough overhaul? Justin Wu: Cost issues. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: I can tell he's frustrated.;<> //電梯跳出提是音效 Justin Wu: Now we are entering the submerged zone. Justin Wu: The tank is not at 100% capacity yet, meaning the Central Control is not entirely submerged.;<> Justin Wu: Next stop is the Cellar.;<> <<#End>>
